urn:contentItem:6JK1-XSF3-SHYX-40ND-00000-00SC JOHNSON & SON - UPDATES FROM COLGATE-PALMOLIVE COMPANY AND PEERS: RECENT INSIGHTS2026-05-13T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:18:41ZLexisNexisSTOCKSC JOHNSON & SON - UPDATES FROM COLGATE-PALMOLIVE COMPANY AND PEERS: RECENT INSIGHTS

DASHBOARD: SC JOHNSON & SON

PermID4298009420Websitehttp://www.scjohnson.com/en/home.aspx?directLink=yIndustryManufacturer of household products

ACTIVITIES:

SC Johnson & Son, founded in 1886 in Racine, Wisconsin, is a family-owned consumer goods company. With $11 billion in revenue and 13,000 employees, it manufactures household brands like Windex, Glade, and Ziploc, operating in 70 countries. SC Johnson emphasizes sustainability, using renewable energy and reducing plastic waste. Its innovation center develops eco-friendly products, and its transparency in ingredient disclosure sets industry standards. Led by H. Fisk Johnson, the companys philanthropy includes land conservation and education grants. SC Johnsons commitment to quality and environmental responsibility reinforces its global leadership in household goods.

+ SC JOHNSON & SON [3 News That Matters]

+ SC JOHNSON ROLLS OUT OUTDOOR INNOVATIONS WITH OFF! AND RAID; SPACE BAG AND BABYGANICS MOVES UNDERWAY

SC Johnson & Son is expanding its outdoor portfolio with new OFF! and Raid products to support spring and summer activities. The announcement, dated 7th May 2026, highlights a line designed to keep patios and gardens bug-free, with prices starting at $5.In parallel, the company disclosed an agreement to acquire the global Space Bag brand from ITW, expected to finalize in coming months pending U.S. regulatory approvals, with financial terms not disclosed.SC Johnson has also acquired Babyganics, founded in 2004, a family-focused brand offering skin care, bath care, insect repellent, and cleaning products, aligning with its strategy to strengthen presence in family and personal care. These moves position the company to meet evolving consumer needs.

+ SC JOHNSON TIES UP WITH EARTHECHO TO SCALE YOUTH ADVOCACY AGAINST PLASTIC POLLUTION

SC Johnson & Son and EarthEcho International announced on March 31, 2026 that the Marine Plastics Ambassadors program will expand to its largest cohort in the three-year collaboration to curb plastic waste.The program will provide advocacy training and opportunities for policy engagement, along with mentorship to help participants design scalable solutions for their local communities."The Marine Plastics Ambassadors are leading the way, and EarthEcho is proud to build a community of practice that empowers them to change the world," said Philippe Cousteau, Jr.Participants will gather in Washington, D.C., this summer to advance policy priorities and engage with legislators, demonstrating the effectiveness of youth-driven solutions. With over 430 million tons of plastic produced globally each year, and projections that plastic entering oceans could nearly triple by 2040, the partnership underscores the urgency of addressing plastic waste.

+ SC JOHNSON UNVEILS DEEP WOODS MAX AEROSOL TARGETED AT OUTDOOR ENTHUSIASTS

SC Johnson and Son launched the Deep Woods MAX aerosol in the OFF! Deep Woods line, a picaridin-based formula that repels mosquitoes and ticks for up to 10 hours, announced on 2nd April 2026.The product is fragrance-free, non-greasy, and safe to use around outdoor equipment such as fishing lines, sporting gear, and painted surfaces.This rollout coincides with a sponsorship making OFF! the official mosquito and tick repellent of Major League Fishing for 2026, including a sponsored angler team in the four 2026 MLF Team Series events.SC Johnson expects the collaboration to boost brand awareness among outdoor enthusiasts as the market for outdoor activities grows.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ COLGATE-PALMOLIVE [3 News That Matters]

+ COLGATE-PALMOLIVE COMPANY (NYSE:CL) ANNOUNCES EVERY COLGATE SMILE HAS A STORY CAMPAIGN IN INDIA

Colgate-Palmolive Company (NYSE:CL) announced on 6th May 2026 the launch of Every Colgate Smile Has A Story by Colgate-Palmolive (India) Limited, a storytelling campaign that marks the brand's 90-year legacy in India.The initiative highlights the bond between Indian mothers and Colgate, with the Colgate toothpaste tube depicted as a trusted family member.The first film Raised by Sweets follows Vertica Khandelwal’s Dehradun family, who have crafted sweets for 125 years, as she shares treats with her children while relying on Colgate’s protective benefits.The Arginine Calcium Boost Technology provides 24-hour cavity protection, and Colgate-Palmolive (India) Limited plans to roll out the series across television, digital platforms and social channels while supporting sustainability programs.

+ COLGATE-PALMOLIVE COMPANY (NYSE:CL) CELEBRATES 90 YEARS IN INDIA WITH CAMPAIGN EMPHASIZING TRUST AND INNOVATION

Colgate-Palmolive Company (NYSE:CL) celebrates 90 years in India with the 'Every Colgate Smile Has A Story' campaign, launched on the 6th of May, 2026, highlighting emotional connections within families and reaffirming leadership in oral health solutions.The campaign captures real-life narratives, including a story from Dehradun about balancing heritage with dental health and the reassurance provided by the slogan 'Colgate Sambhal Lega'.Colgate-Palmolive (India) Limited continues to lead the sector by pairing emotional resonance with scientific integrity, driven by Arginine + Calcium Boost Technology delivering 24-hour cavity protection.By connecting with families and highlighting scientific advances, Colgate-Palmolive reinforces its commitment to trusted oral health solutions in India's market.

+ COLGATE-PALMOLIVE COMPANY (NYSE:CL) EXPANDS BRAND NARRATIVE IN INDIA WITH EMOTION-DRIVEN MOTHER-FOCUSED SERIES

Colgate-Palmolive Company (NYSE:CL) Introduces the Real Stories Platform Through Colgate-Palmolive (India) Limited to Highlight Family Health in India, launching on 4th May 2026 to weave real consumer narratives into brand messaging and reflect a shift from traditional advertising toward emotional connections built over Colgate's nearly 90-year presence in Indian homes. The first film, 'Raised by Sweets,' features Vertica Khandelwal from Dehradun as she balances motherhood within a 125-year tradition of sweet making, highlighting dental health concerns and reinforcing the message that Colgate Sambhal Lega will handle it. Strong Teeth, powered by Arginine + Calcium Boost Technology, provides 24-hour cavity protection as the series uses emotional storytelling backed by science. Gunjit Jain, Executive Vice President – Marketing at Colgate-Palmolive India Limited, notes that the campaign honors the trust mothers place in the brand and aims to extend its reach across TV, digital, and social channels as Colgate positions itself as a Suraksha Chakra in family life.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ PROCTER AND GAMBLE [3 News That Matters]

+ THE PROCTER & GAMBLE COMPANY (NYSE:PG) EARNS TOP RANKING IN HOUSEHOLD PRODUCTS ON FORTUNE'S 2026 INNOVATION LIST

The Procter & Gamble Company (NYSE:PG) earned the top spot in the Household Products Industry on Fortune's 2026 America's Most Innovative Companies list, marking a three-year lead. The Fortune ranking, created with Statista, evaluates Product Innovation, Process Innovation and Innovation Culture across 300 leading U.S. innovators, with a combined revenue exceeding $12.5 trillion. The announcement was made on 23rd April 2026. Victor Aguilar, Chief Research, Development & Innovation Officer at The Procter & Gamble Company (NYSE:PG), stated, "This recognition is a testament to our incredible organization around the world and their commitment to pushing the boundaries of what's possible, transforming how we innovate." He highlighted the consumer-first approach, leveraging advanced science and technology to develop solutions that resonate with customers. Procter & Gamble continues to drive innovation across Fabric Care, Home Care and Personal Health Care, deploying its intellectual property portfolio to convert scientific advances into products that enhance consumer lives. The company counts Tide, Gillette and Olay among its brands and serves consumers in approximately 70 countries. For more information, visit https://www.pg.com

+ MR. CLEAN RETURNS WITH PRO LINE TO ADDRESS WORKPLACE CLEANING CHALLENGES

CINCINNATI—Mr. Clean is back in the professional cleaning arena with Mr. Clean PRO, a line designed for business use. Today, 10th April 2026, P&G PRO unveiled products aimed at delivering dependable, efficient cleaning for operations ranging from restaurants to offices.Cleaning is a critical operation across sectors, and the PRO line targets grease, stains and grime with commercial-grade formulas. The range includes the Mr. Clean PRO Commercial All Surface Cleaner and the Deep Cleaner with Bleach, built to perform in busy environments.Bryan Smith, Vice President North America at P&G PRO, emphasized that the right cleaner can make a big difference for professionals. The products are available nationwide through distributors and retailers in sizes from 1 gallon to 32 oz, with more information at pgpro.com and on P&G PRO's social channels.

+ PROCTER & GAMBLE DEPLOYS MULTI-BRAND WNBA PARTNERSHIP TO STRENGTHEN RELEVANCE

Procter & Gamble has formed a multi-year, multi-brand partnership with the WNBA to expand engagement with women's basketball and its growing audience, including athlete collaborations, league events, and digital storytelling.Revealed on the 9th of April, 2026, the initiative emphasizes emotional connections and brand relevance over simple product placement, aiming to attract younger and more diverse consumers.Investors will monitor whether the campaigns improve brand equity and loyalty, with P&G’s sports and entertainment spending guided by the execution and scale of the initiatives.While optimistic, investors weigh P&G's financial health, noting the shares trade about 13% below expectations and have declined roughly 6.6% in the last month, which may affect timing and disclosure needs.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ RB [3 News That Matters]

+ RECKITT BENCKISER GROUP PLC (L:RKT) REPORTS 80% OF CHINA SALES FROM E COMMERCE AS DIGITAL SHIFT ACCELERATES

Reckitt Benckiser Group PLC (L:RKT) is navigating a pivotal moment as it adapts to China's fast-evolving e-commerce landscape. Kris Licht, CEO of Reckitt, describes the transition as the most profound channel shift he has encountered. As of 9th May 2026, e-commerce and social commerce accounted for roughly 80% of China sales, up from 30% six years ago, and the region's revenue is nearing £1 billion after 11 consecutive quarters of double-digit growth. The shift accompanies Reckitt's push toward digital sales initiatives, accelerated product cycles, and the integration of artificial intelligence. The company will host its Focus On: Digital Science virtual investor event on 14th May to illustrate how its digital and R&D teams are leveraging technology to drive innovation and category growth in China. Reckitt also disclosed a buyback of 190,500 ordinary shares totaling about £9 million as part of capital returns. Yet the first-quarter revenue missed expectations and management forecast a dip in first-half margins. Analysts described the quarter as broadly muted in growth and questioned the ability to meet full-year targets, particularly in emerging markets. Russia sanctions add complexity to Reckitt's operations there, with the local arm pursuing new product development and IP filings to fill gaps caused by the restrictions. Licht remains optimistic that accelerated digital moves could mitigate some challenges, but investors will be watching whether the digital strategy translates into sales growth beyond China.

+ RECKITT BENCKISER GROUP PLC (L:RKT) DRIVES INNOVATION IN AIR FRESHENERS AMID MARKET FORECAST OF $19.75 BILLION BY 2030

Reckitt Benckiser Group PLC (L:RKT) and peers in the air fresheners market position themselves for growth as forecasts point to $19.75 billion by 2030. As of 27th April 2026, the market is set to rise from $14.36 billion in 2025 to $15.43 billion in 2026, a 7.5% compound annual growth rate.The sector is driven by urbanization and a rising focus on indoor ambiance and wellness. Reckitt Benckiser Group PLC (L:RKT) and rivals like Godrej Consumer Products and Procter & Gamble actively develop fragrance solutions to capture growing consumer interest. Innovations include aerosol dispersion technology such as Everspray micro-diffusion systems delivering ultra fine droplets with minimal waste.Europe led the market in 2025, while Asia-Pacific is projected to be the fastest growing region in the coming years. Investors monitor how the group monetizes the opportunity as demand broadens and distribution expands.

+ RECKITT BENCKISER GROUP PLC (L:RKT) HARRY KIRSCH TAKES SEAT ON GALDERMA BOARD

Reckitt Benckiser Group plc (L:RKT) announced that Harry Kirsch, one of its non-executive directors, was appointed to the Galderma board (SIX:GALD) effective 28th April 2026. Kirsch brings governance experience to a Swiss-based firm focused on skincare and dermatology.The appointment was disclosed by Reckitt Benckiser in accordance with Listing Rule 9.6.14.Kirsch will remain a non-executive director at Reckitt Benckiser, and the terms of his Galderma appointment were not disclosed.The dual roles could support Galderma's strategic direction and create opportunities in product development and markets for both companies.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ UNILEVER [3 News That Matters]

+ UNILEVER PLC (L:ULVR) AND MCCORMICK & CO. (NYSE:MKC) COMBINE FOOD BUSINESSES IN A $44.8 BILLION DEAL

Unilever PLC (L:ULVR) and McCormick & Co. (NYSE:MKC) have agreed to combine their food businesses in a transaction valued at $44.8 billion, blending $15.7 billion in cash with $29.1 billion of McCormick stock. Unilever will own 65% of the new company, while McCormick holds the remainder. The merged portfolio will include brands such as French's mustard and Hellmann's mayonnaise, aligning with each company's strategy to focus on core strengths. The parties disclosed the transaction on 11th May 2026. The deal marks the largest transaction in the histories of both firms and aims to extend scale and reach in global food markets, with careful integration required to realize potential synergies

+ UNILEVER PLC (L:ULVR) INVESTORS PUSH FOR DEFORESTATION FREE SOURCING IN MCCORMICK DEAL

Unilever plans to merge its food business with McCormick in a $65 billion deal unveiled in March, creating a global platform that includes Hellmann's and Cholula. The merger, aimed to take effect as of 8th May 2026, would expand McCormick's scale and complicate its supply chain across farming and smallholders. Investors press for deforestation-free commitments and full supply chain traceability. Storebrand’s Vemund Olsen says the merged group should uphold best practices in commodity sourcing. With Storebrand among the top holders of both companies, stakeholders emphasize sustainable sourcing as a core risk management issue. Unilever is expected to hold about 10% and secure four board seats in the merged company, preserving influence while giving smaller investors less room to shape strategy. McCormick's sustainability program remains under scrutiny; Sustainalytics notes medium-risk exposure and the lack of a formal no-deforestation pledge, with details on tracing and auditing still pending. McCormick says progress hinges on stronger supplier engagement and better data, and it is pursuing a strategic update process for its sustainability program.

+ UNILEVER PLC (LSE:ULVR) REVIEWS FOOD BUSINESS DIVESTMENT OR SPIN-OFF AMID GROWTH SHIFT

Unilever PLC (LSE:ULVR) is weighing a separation of its food assets as part of a broader strategy to focus on beauty, personal care and wellbeing brands. On 29th April 2026, sources familiar with the discussions said the group is working with advisers to assess options, including a potential divestment of the food business, which includes Hellmann's mayonnaise and Knorr stock cubes. Deliberations cover a full spin-off or selective divestments, with a formal decision not expected before 2027. The food portfolio also includes Colman's condiments and Marmite, and the shares have traded about 0.7% lower, keeping Unilever's market capitalization near £107 billion ($142 billion). Under CEO Fernando Fernandez, the company continues to pivot toward its beauty and wellbeing brands, having spun off the ice cream division last year while retaining a minority stake to be sold gradually. Analysts see a clear direction toward beauty, but note that timing for a food separation could be sensitive given current market conditions. Unilever has previously divested its global spreads division and snack brands, leaving roughly €1 billion to €1.5 billion of local food brands still to exit, as the company refines its focus on core brands such as Dove and Liquid IV

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

urn:contentItem:6JH5-H343-SGWP-J0N5-00000-00Wisconsin candidate offers realistic data center solution | Letter2026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:11:39ZLexisNexisCONSTRUCTION NEWS & MILWAUKEE NEWSWisconsin candidate offers realistic data center solution | LetterLetters to the Editor, Milwaukee Journal Sentinel

Referencing the article “Governor candidates speak on data centers,” (April 17) a number of proposals were offered: Statewide freeze on construction until “guard rails” are in place (Francesca Hong, Joel Brennan); End subsidies, sales tax exemption, TIF districts and use of productive farmland for data centers (Tom Tiffany); require environmental and utility impact study before each new project (Mandela Barnes); allow local residents to have veto power over construction (Tiffany, Hong, Sara Rodriguez, Kelda Roys). All oppose nondisclosure agreements for these centers. All want to keep increased utility costs down for local ratepayers. A lot of agreement.

But here’s an important distinction, Rodriguez has stated that each data center should produce 100% of their own power onsite, and that it should be with green power (geo-thermal, solar, wind) rather than by fossil fuel. This is significant because onsite access eliminates long-distance energy infrastructure.

American Transmission Company is currently asking the Public Service Commission's permission to build a $1.5 billion transmission line and substations of 345-kilovolts (kV) (at ratepayers’ expense), cutting a 96-123 mile path, 100 feet wide, through Wisconsin forests, wetlands, farmland and neighborhoods in order for a combination of renewable and gas-burning power to reach the Port Washington campus, along with diesel engine back-up power.

Another ATC proposal is in early phases for Mount Pleasant. Considering these permanent environmental impacts, Rodriguez’s requirement for onsite energy seems the only realistic solution to preventing disastrous destruction of our natural resources in order to drive data centers — which some say may become obsolete within a decade.

Dianne Dagelen, Wauwatosa

Here are some tips to get your views shared with your friends, family, neighbors and across our state:

Please include your name, street address and daytime phone.

Generally, we limit letters to 200 words.

Cite sources of where you found information or the article that prompted your letter.

Be civil and constructive, especially when criticizing.

Avoid ad hominem attacks, take issue with a position, not a person.

We cannot acknowledge receipt of submissions.

We don't publish poetry, anonymous or open letters.

Each writer is limited to one published letter every two months.

All letters are subject to editing.

Write: Letters to the editor, Milwaukee Journal Sentinel, 330 E. Kilbourn Avenue, Suite 500, Milwaukee, WI, 53202. Fax: (414)-223-5444. E-mail: jsedit@jrn.com or submit using the form that can be found on the on the bottom of this page.

This article originally appeared on Milwaukee Journal Sentinel: Wisconsin candidate offers realistic data center solution | Letter

urn:contentItem:6JJF-9X33-S406-14DB-00000-00Will Alliant Energy Corporation's (NASDAQ:LNT), Recent rise Hold Strong for Traders?2026-05-11T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:18:41ZLexisNexisWill Alliant Energy Corporation's (NASDAQ:LNT), Recent rise Hold Strong for Traders?

NASDAQ STOCK REPORT

MADISON, Friday, May 08 2026 - Ahead of this morning's market opening, Alliant Energy Corporation closed higher the previous trading day. Alliant Energy Corporation (NASDAQ:LNT), the NASDAQ's 5th largest Utility company by market cap, increased 0.1% during its previous trading session to close at $71.76.

Alliant Energy Corporation (NASDAQ:LNT) is a leading utility provider focused on sustaining growth through strategic initiatives in renewable energy and infrastructure. The company is driving an innovative pivot towards powering AI data centers, highlighted by the Beaver Dam AI Data Center initiative. This strategic alliance with Meta emphasizes its adaptability to industrial shifts, with Alliant Energy's power being crucial for Midwest data centers. Long-term competitive strengths include a resilient infrastructure, stability in the utility market, and ongoing investments in wind projects, like the new wind project in Minnesota, which enhance their renewable energy portfolio and offer steady returns amid inflation pressures.

Tue Apr 21, 2026: Alliant Energy Advances Bent Tree North Wind Project in Minnesota

Alliant Energy has announced that its Bent Tree North Wind project in Freeborn County, Minnesota, will proceed following recent regulatory approvals. The energy company received unanimous backing for the construction of a 150-megawatt (MW) project adjacent to its existing 200-MW Bent Tree wind farm. This approval was granted by both the Minnesota Public Utilities Commission (PUC) and the Public Service Commission of Wisconsin (PSC) on the 21st of April, 2026, marking a significant milestone for Alliant Energy.

Becky Valcq, president of Alliant Energy's Wisconsin energy company, emphasized that the unanimous approvals from regulators in both states reflect strong confidence in the project's potential benefits. "Approval of the Bent Tree North Wind project marks an important step as Alliant Energy continues to build a balanced energy portfolio that includes both renewable and traditional resources to meet customer demand," Valcq stated.

The Bent Tree North Wind project has garnered support from various stakeholders, including the Freeborn County Chamber of Commerce and multiple labor unions. These endorsements highlight the project's potential to stimulate economic growth in the region and enhance the reliability of the energy grid. Valerie Lockhart, executive director of the Albert Lea-Freeborn County Chamber of Commerce, noted, "The Bent Tree North Wind Project represents continued, meaningful investment in Freeborn County—creating jobs, supporting our tax base, and delivering long-term value to local landowners."

Alliant Energy projects that the new wind initiative will generate over $100 million in economic benefits, covering local tax revenue, payments to landowners, construction wages, and other community investments. The company's projections indicate that the project will produce sufficient renewable energy to power approximately 50,000 homes annually, reinforcing its commitment to sustainable energy solutions.

The Bent Tree North initiative is a key component of Alliant Energy's broader Energy Blueprint, which outlines the company's strategy for integrating renewable and traditional energy generation resources. This roadmap aims to provide cost-effective and reliable energy to customers while promoting a balanced energy future. For further details about the Bent Tree North Wind project and Alliant Energy's wind energy initiatives, visit the company's official website.

Tue Apr 14, 2026: Alliant Energy's Strategic Moves Position it for Growth in a Shifting Economy

Alliant Energy Corporation is poised for resilient growth as industrial growth shifts toward data centers and infrastructure. This is particularly relevant for U.S. investors looking for defensive investments with built-in inflation protection. On 14th April 2026, Alliant Energy's regulated business model positions the company favorably to meet America's energy demands.

Alliant Energy Corporation, identified by the stock code US0188021085, provides a stake in a regulated utility that supplies electricity and natural gas primarily in Iowa and Wisconsin. Its regulated structure means that the company earns returns sanctioned by state regulatory commissions, shielding it from the economic fluctuations that often impact discretionary sectors more severely. For investors, this translates into a stable business model characterized by predictable cash flows derived from essential services rather than volatile commodity prices.

The utility generates power from a well-balanced mix of coal, natural gas, wind, and solar energy, and it continues to invest in renewable sources to meet environmental mandates. This diversification lessens the risks associated with fuel costs and positions Alliant for federal incentives tied to decarbonization efforts. Historically, U.S. utilities have offered dividends in the range of 3-4%, which adds to their appeal for long-term investors.

Shifts in industrial technology are creating favorable conditions for utilities such as Alliant. Sectors like data centers and infrastructure are becoming increasingly significant consumers of power. The global industrial tech market is projected to experience robust growth, particularly in automation and green technology, which are expected to grow at double-digit CAGRs. This rising demand for reliable baseload power corresponds well with Alliant's capabilities, particularly as hyperscale data centers expand.

Going forward, it will be important for investors to keep an eye on upcoming rate case outcomes and quarterly reports indicating load growth, as well as dividend announcements that reinforce income strategies. Relevant macroeconomic indicators, including Federal Reserve interest rate paths and infrastructure bill implementations, could either strengthen or weaken Alliant's market position.

Alliant Energy Corp. is strategically positioning itself to address the surging electricity demand from data centers and industrial infrastructure across the Midwest. The company currently manages its energy generation through a balanced portfolio that includes wind, solar, natural gas, and coal. Ongoing capital plans are focused on grid modernization and expanding renewable energy resources to comply with clean energy mandates and capitalize on federal decarbonization incentives.

SECTION 5 CORPORATE PROFILE

5.1 Activities

Alliant Energy Corporation is a publicly traded utility holding company listed on the NASDAQ, operating primarily in the Midwest United States through its subsidiaries, Interstate Power and Light Company and Wisconsin Power and Light Company. Its core business activities center on the generation, transmission, distribution, and sale of electricity and natural gas, serving approximately 1 million electric and 420,000 natural gas customers in Wisconsin and Iowa. The company emphasizes reliable energy delivery while prioritizing environmental sustainability and infrastructure modernization. Among its fastest-growing segments are renewable energy initiatives, particularly wind and solar power generation, which have expanded rapidly due to increasing regulatory support, technological advancements, and rising demand for clean energy sources. This strategic focus positions Alliant Energy to enhance its competitive edge in the evolving utility sector, with ongoing investments aimed at reducing carbon emissions and improving operational efficiency. It is the NASDAQ's 5th largest Utility company by market capitalisation.

Alliant Energy Corporation (NASDAQ:LNT) is a leading supplier in the Midwest data center energy market, offering a comprehensive range of services tailored for data centers. With Alliant Energy data center power services, the company is strategically positioned to provide green power for data centers through its commitment to carbon-free data center power and renewable energy credits (RECs). Alliant Energy's data center electrification projects enhance its portfolio with cutting-edge energy-as-a-service for data centers. Their focus on Alliant Energy grid modernization for data center growth reflects their progressive approach toward sustainability. As a top-tier Midwest utility, they deliver Alliant Energy electricity supply to Iowa and Wisconsin, distinguishing themselves with high-density power solutions and reliability. Alliant Energy corporate power solutions and Alliant Energy colocation power partnerships further solidify their market leadership. Investors should note Alliant Energy's strategic advancements in transmission and capacity upgrades, ensuring robust power reliability for data centers.

urn:contentItem:6JHV-2T23-S4NP-P074-00000-00Quarterly Report Analysis Quarter ending 31 March 2026: WEC Energy EPS soars 155% Corporate Wire Date: 06 May 2026 12:37 EDT Release Date: May 05, 20262026-05-08T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:18:41ZLexisNexisQuarterly Report Analysis Quarter ending 31 March 2026: WEC Energy EPS soars 155% Corporate Wire Date: 06 May 2026 12:37 EDT Release Date: May 05, 2026

AMERICAN RESULTS

WEC Energy (NYSE:WEC), announced EPS of $2.47 for the quarter-ended 31 March 2026 [Q1/2026], up 155% sequentially from 97.0c in the previous quarter [Q4/2025].

Major changes compared with previous corresponding period [Mar 31, 2026 vs Mar 31, 2025]:

Favourable Changes:

- Net profit was up 11.1% from $724.2m to $804.4m

- Total revenue was up 9% from $3.1b to $3.4b

- EPS growth 8.3% from $2.28 to $2.47

- Profit after tax to Revenues was up from 23% to 23.4%

Unfavourable Changes:

- EBITDA to Revenues was down from 36.4% to 36.1%

Note:

- Depreciation to Revenues was down from 11.4% to 11.1%

Quarterly Report (Quarter ending 31 Mar 2026)

Compared with the Previous Corresponding Period [PCP; Q1/2025], year-over-year [y.o.y.] EPS was up 8.3%, Revenue was up 9% and Net Profit was up 11.1%.

ACTIVITIES:

WEC Energy Group, Inc., a Fortune 500 company listed on the New York Stock Exchange, is a leading energy services provider in the United States, primarily operating in Wisconsin and surrounding regions. Its core business activities encompass the generation, transmission, and distribution of electricity, along with natural gas delivery, serving over 4.6 million customers through subsidiaries like We Energies and Wisconsin Public Service. The company focuses on reliable, affordable, and sustainable energy solutions, with a diversified portfolio that includes fossil fuels, nuclear, and renewables. Among its fastest-growing segments are renewable energy initiatives, such as wind and solar projects, which have expanded rapidly due to increasing demand for clean power, supportive regulations, and corporate sustainability goals. This growth is evidenced by significant investments in wind farms and solar installations, positioning WEC Energy to meet rising environmental standards while enhancing long-term operational efficiency and shareholder value.

MARKET POSITION:

WEC Energy Group, Inc (NYSE:WEC), the NYSE's 7th largest Utility company by market cap, climbed $10.07 (or 9.6%) in the trailing 12 months to May 05, 2026 to close at $115.22. Compared with the S&P 500 Index which has risen 27.6% over the same period, this is a relative price change of -18.1%. Average daily volume in the trailing 12 months equalled average trading of 2.1 million shares in the previous year.

Note:

urn:contentItem:6JHM-XJ53-RSYN-91F6-00000-00Quarterly Report Analysis Quarter ending 31 March 2026: WEC Energy EPS soars 155% Corporate Wire Date: 06 May 2026 12:37 EDT Release Date: May 05, 20262026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:18:41ZLexisNexisQuarterly Report Analysis Quarter ending 31 March 2026: WEC Energy EPS soars 155% Corporate Wire Date: 06 May 2026 12:37 EDT Release Date: May 05, 2026

AMERICAN RESULTS

WEC Energy (NYSE:WEC), announced EPS of $2.47 for the quarter-ended 31 March 2026 [Q1/2026], up 155% sequentially from 97.0c in the previous quarter [Q4/2025].

Major changes compared with previous corresponding period [Mar 31, 2026 vs Mar 31, 2025]:

Favourable Changes:

- Net profit was up 11.1% from $724.2m to $804.4m

- Total revenue was up 9% from $3.1b to $3.4b

- EPS growth 8.3% from $2.28 to $2.47

- Profit after tax to Revenues was up from 23% to 23.4%

Unfavourable Changes:

- EBITDA to Revenues was down from 36.4% to 36.1%

Note:

- Depreciation to Revenues was down from 11.4% to 11.1%

Quarterly Report (Quarter ending 31 Mar 2026)

Quarter-ended31 Mar 202631 Dec 202530 Sep 2025EPS$2.597c84cSequential growth in EPS %155159.1Revenue, $ billion3.42.52.1Sequential growth in Revenue %35.420.64.7Net Profit, $ million804317271Sequential growth in Net Profit %15416.710.6

Compared with the Previous Corresponding Period [PCP; Q1/2025], year-over-year [y.o.y.] EPS was up 8.3%, Revenue was up 9% and Net Profit was up 11.1%.

Quarter-ended31 Mar 202631 Mar 2025EPS, $2.52.3PCP growth in EPS %8.316Revenue, $ billion3.43.1PCP growth in Revenue %9.017.5Net Profit, $ million804724PCP growth in Net Profit %11.116.4

ACTIVITIES:

WEC Energy Group, Inc., a Fortune 500 company listed on the New York Stock Exchange, is a leading energy services provider in the United States, primarily operating in Wisconsin and surrounding regions. Its core business activities encompass the generation, transmission, and distribution of electricity, along with natural gas delivery, serving over 4.6 million customers through subsidiaries like We Energies and Wisconsin Public Service. The company focuses on reliable, affordable, and sustainable energy solutions, with a diversified portfolio that includes fossil fuels, nuclear, and renewables. Among its fastest-growing segments are renewable energy initiatives, such as wind and solar projects, which have expanded rapidly due to increasing demand for clean power, supportive regulations, and corporate sustainability goals. This growth is evidenced by significant investments in wind farms and solar installations, positioning WEC Energy to meet rising environmental standards while enhancing long-term operational efficiency and shareholder value.

MARKET POSITION:

WEC Energy Group, Inc (NYSE:WEC), the NYSE's 7th largest Utility company by market cap, climbed $10.07 (or 9.6%) in the trailing 12 months to May 05, 2026 to close at $115.22. Compared with the S&P 500 Index which has risen 27.6% over the same period, this is a relative price change of -18.1%. Average daily volume in the trailing 12 months equalled average trading of 2.1 million shares in the previous year.

May 4th, 2026 ( Transcript Daily - Delivered by Newstex )

MGE Energy ( NASDAQ:MGEE - Get Free Report) released its quarterly earnings results on Tuesday. The utilities provider reported $1.32 earnings per share for the quarter, topping the consensus estimate of $1.13 by $0.19, FiscalAI reports. MGE Energy had a net margin of 18.27% and a return on equity of 10.60%. The business had revenue of $242.70 million for the quarter, compared to the consensus estimate of $227.06 million.

MGE Energy Price Performance

NASDAQ MGEE traded up $0.45 on Tuesday, reaching $80.56. The company had a trading volume of 42,143 shares, compared to its average volume of 171,105. The company has a quick ratio of 0.55, a current ratio of 0.77 and a debt-to-equity ratio of 0.61. The stock has a market capitalization of $2.96 billion, a PE ratio of 21.65 and a beta of 0.76. The firm's 50 day moving average price is $78.47 and its 200 day moving average price is $80.10. MGE Energy has a 12 month low of $72.17 and a 12 month high of $94.00.

Insider Transactions at MGE Energy

In related news, Director James G. Berbee purchased 373 shares of MGE Energy stock in a transaction on Wednesday, March 11th. The shares were bought at an average cost of $77.74 per share, for a total transaction of $28,997.02. Following the acquisition, the director owned 9,362 shares in the company, valued at approximately $727,801.88. The trade was a 4.15% increase in their position. The transaction was disclosed in a document filed with the Securities & Exchange Commission, which is available through this hyperlink. Corporate insiders own 0.41% of the company's stock.

Hedge Funds Weigh In On MGE Energy

A number of institutional investors and hedge funds have recently made changes to their positions in MGEE. Advisory Services Network LLC purchased a new position in shares of MGE Energy in the 3rd quarter worth $40,000. Parallel Advisors LLC boosted its stake in shares of MGE Energy by 28.6% during the 3rd quarter. Parallel Advisors LLC now owns 714 shares of the utilities provider's stock worth $60,000 after purchasing an additional 159 shares during the period. UMB Bank n.a. boosted its stake in shares of MGE Energy by 807.2% during the 4th quarter. UMB Bank n.a. now owns 1,261 shares of the utilities provider's stock worth $99,000 after purchasing an additional 1,122 shares during the period. Kestra Advisory Services LLC purchased a new position in shares of MGE Energy during the 4th quarter worth about $100,000. Finally, Smartleaf Asset Management LLC boosted its stake in shares of MGE Energy by 19.1% during the 2nd quarter. Smartleaf Asset Management LLC now owns 1,326 shares of the utilities provider's stock worth $117,000 after purchasing an additional 213 shares during the period. 52.58% of the stock is owned by hedge funds and other institutional investors.

Analysts Set New Price Targets

Several research analysts have weighed in on the stock. Wall Street Zen upgraded shares of MGE Energy from a "sell" rating to a "hold" rating in a research report on Sunday, March 8th. Morgan Stanley restated an "underweight" rating on shares of MGE Energy in a research report on Monday, April 27th. Two analysts have rated the stock with a Hold rating and one has given a Sell rating to the stock. Based on data from MarketBeat.com, MGE Energy currently has an average rating of "Reduce" and a consensus price target of $80.50.

Read Our Latest Report on MGE Energy

MGE Energy Company Profile

( Get Free Report)

MGE Energy, Inc is a public utility holding company headquartered in Madison, Wisconsin. Through its principal subsidiary, Madison Gas and Electric Company (MGE), the company provides regulated electric and natural gas distribution services to residential, commercial and industrial customers in south-central Wisconsin. MGE Energy's operations encompass the generation, transmission and distribution of electricity as well as the purchase and distribution of natural gas, with an emphasis on reliable service and system resilience.

Since reorganizing as a holding company in 2001, MGE Energy has expanded its portfolio to include nonregulated subsidiaries involved in renewable energy development, energy services and infrastructure support.

AMERICAN RESULTS

WEC Energy (NYSE:WEC), announced net profit of $804m for the quarter-ended 31 March 2026 [Q1/2026], up 154% from the previous quarter [Q4/2025] and up 11% from the year-earlier period [Q1/2025].

Major changes compared with previous corresponding period [Mar 31, 2026 vs Mar 31, 2025]:

Favourable Changes:

- Net profit was up 11.1% from $724.2m to $804.4m

- Total revenue was up 9% from $3.1b to $3.4b

- EPS growth 8.3% from $2.28 to $2.47

- Profit after tax to Revenues was up from 23% to 23.4%

Unfavourable Changes:

- EBITDA to Revenues was down from 36.4% to 36.1%

Note:

- Depreciation to Revenues was down from 11.4% to 11.1%

ACTIVITIES:

WEC Energy Group, Inc., a Fortune 500 company listed on the New York Stock Exchange, is a leading energy services provider in the United States, primarily operating in Wisconsin and surrounding regions. Its core business activities encompass the generation, transmission, and distribution of electricity, along with natural gas delivery, serving over 4.6 million customers through subsidiaries like We Energies and Wisconsin Public Service. The company focuses on reliable, affordable, and sustainable energy solutions, with a diversified portfolio that includes fossil fuels, nuclear, and renewables. Among its fastest-growing segments are renewable energy initiatives, such as wind and solar projects, which have expanded rapidly due to increasing demand for clean power, supportive regulations, and corporate sustainability goals. This growth is evidenced by significant investments in wind farms and solar installations, positioning WEC Energy to meet rising environmental standards while enhancing long-term operational efficiency and shareholder value.

MARKET POSITION:

WEC Energy Group, Inc (NYSE:WEC), the NYSE's 7th largest Utility company by market cap, climbed $10.07 (or 9.6%) in the trailing 12 months to May 05, 2026 to close at $115.22. Compared with the S&P 500 Index which has risen 27.6% over the same period, this is a relative price change of -18.1%. Alliant Energy Corporation (NASDAQ:LNT), the NASDAQ's 5th largest Utility company by market cap, dropped 1.0% during its previous trading session to close at $72.31.

Tue Apr 21, 2026: Alliant Energy Advances Bent Tree North Wind Project in Minnesota

Alliant Energy has announced that its Bent Tree North Wind project in Freeborn County, Minnesota, will proceed following recent regulatory approvals. The energy company received unanimous backing for the construction of a 150-megawatt (MW) project adjacent to its existing 200-MW Bent Tree wind farm. This approval was granted by both the Minnesota Public Utilities Commission (PUC) and the Public Service Commission of Wisconsin (PSC) on the 21st of April, 2026, marking a significant milestone for Alliant Energy.

Becky Valcq, president of Alliant Energy's Wisconsin energy company, emphasized that the unanimous approvals from regulators in both states reflect strong confidence in the project's potential benefits. "Approval of the Bent Tree North Wind project marks an important step as Alliant Energy continues to build a balanced energy portfolio that includes both renewable and traditional resources to meet customer demand," Valcq stated.

The Bent Tree North Wind project has garnered support from various stakeholders, including the Freeborn County Chamber of Commerce and multiple labor unions. These endorsements highlight the project's potential to stimulate economic growth in the region and enhance the reliability of the energy grid. Valerie Lockhart, executive director of the Albert Lea-Freeborn County Chamber of Commerce, noted, "The Bent Tree North Wind Project represents continued, meaningful investment in Freeborn County—creating jobs, supporting our tax base, and delivering long-term value to local landowners."

Alliant Energy projects that the new wind initiative will generate over $100 million in economic benefits, covering local tax revenue, payments to landowners, construction wages, and other community investments. The company's projections indicate that the project will produce sufficient renewable energy to power approximately 50,000 homes annually, reinforcing its commitment to sustainable energy solutions.

The Bent Tree North initiative is a key component of Alliant Energy's broader Energy Blueprint, which outlines the company's strategy for integrating renewable and traditional energy generation resources. This roadmap aims to provide cost-effective and reliable energy to customers while promoting a balanced energy future. For further details about the Bent Tree North Wind project and Alliant Energy's wind energy initiatives, visit the company's official website.

Recent News 2 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 321 words

Tue Apr 14, 2026: Alliant Energy Sets Sights on Data Center Demand Amid Utility Stability

Alliant Energy Corp. is strategically positioning itself to address the surging electricity demand from data centers and industrial infrastructure across the Midwest. As of 15th April 2026, the utility provider serves over 1 million electric and 400,000 natural gas customers through its primary subsidiaries, Interstate Power and Light Company in Iowa and Wisconsin Power and Light Company in Wisconsin.

With a regulated utility model, Alliant Energy earns returns that are approved by state commissions, providing a buffer against volatile economic cycles. The company currently manages its energy generation through a balanced portfolio that includes wind, solar, natural gas, and coal. Ongoing capital plans are focused on grid modernization and expanding renewable energy resources to comply with clean energy mandates and capitalize on federal decarbonization incentives.

The demand for steady power is projected to rise significantly, driven by an anticipated 6% compound annual growth rate in industrial technology through 2030. According to industry experts, utilities like Alliant Energy anchor their portfolios by concentrating on essential infrastructure, particularly during periods of market uncertainty. The emergence of hyperscaler-driven data centers in the Upper Midwest necessitates reliable baseload power, a demand that aligns with Alliant Energy's regional capabilities.

This structural shift in energy demand supports Alliant Energy's disciplined capital approach, which aims for an annual earnings growth of 6% to 8%. Analysts generally hold a favorable view of the company due to its rate base growth and a consistent track record of dividends. Despite concerns regarding interest rate sensitivity, Alliant Energy's low-leverage strategy is designed to outpace inflation while ensuring service reliability, further solidifying its position in the market.

As Alliant Energy continues to navigate the evolving energy landscape, its focus on data center growth not only enhances its utility stability but also positions the company for sustainable long-term success. With a commitment to meeting the energy needs of the future, Alliant Energy is poised to play a critical role in the region's economic development.

Recent News 3 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 363 words

Tue Apr 14, 2026: Alliant Energy's New Strategy Focuses on Powering Data Centers in the Midwest

Alliant Energy Corporation has announced a strategic pivot aimed at addressing the growing electricity demands of hyperscale data centers in the Midwest. On the 13th of April 2026, the company revealed plans to leverage its regulated utility model to ensure grid reliability while targeting an ambitious annual earnings growth of 6% to 8%. This shift reflects a broader strategy to modernize the Midwest energy grid, aligning with the increasing demand for robust digital infrastructure.

Serving over 1 million electric and 400,000 natural gas customers through its subsidiaries, Interstate Power and Light and Wisconsin Power and Light, Alliant Energy is responding to a projected 6% compound annual growth rate in industrial technology through 2030. This strategic adaptation allows Alliant Energy to utilize its regulated utility framework—earning state-approved returns—to mitigate economic volatility while fulfilling the heavy baseload power requirements of modern data centers. By managing a balanced generation portfolio that includes wind, solar, natural gas, and coal, the company aims to ensure a sustainable energy future.

The utility's focus on grid modernization is designed to align with both federal decarbonization mandates and the technical needs of high-capacity data facilities. Analysts view this move as positioning Alliant Energy as a defensive investment, offering shareholders predictable dividends amid significant transitions in the energy market. The company's commitment to integrating digital infrastructure into its utility model reflects a proactive approach to the evolving energy landscape.

By prioritizing data center integration, Alliant Energy seeks to influence regional energy pricing and service reliability positively. Reports suggest that by aligning its investment strategies with long-term power needs, Alliant Energy intends to adopt a disciplined capital approach aimed at achieving its targeted annual earnings growth. This strategy not only mitigates interest rate sensitivity but also capitalizes on the increasing demand for reliable, large-scale power in the Upper Midwest.

The shift towards integrating data centers into its utility operations represents a pivotal recalibration for Alliant Energy, indicating that traditional power providers are now recognizing digital infrastructure as a key driver of grid modernization and long-term financial stability. As the landscape of energy generation continues to evolve, Alliant Energy's strategic focus on these high-growth sectors positions it favorably for future developments in the industry.

2.1 Other Bullish Signals

- Return on Equity of 11.0% versus sector average of 8.6%.

MCap/Total Assets:

- Tobin's Q Ratio, defined as MCap divided by Total Assets, is 0.7. Compared with the rest of the market the stock is undervalued.

- Net profit margin has averaged 18.3% in the last 3 years. This is considered superior and suggests a high margin of safety.

- As per the Du Pont analysis, Return on Equity of 11.0% is better than sector average of 8.6%. This is computed as net profit margin of 18.6% times asset turnover [sales/assets] of 0.17 times leverage factor [total assets/shareholders' equity] of 3.4.

SECTION 3 ONGOING BULLISH PARAMETERS

3.1 Alliant Energy Corporation sees dividend rise for an eighth consecutive year

Alliant Energy Corporation reported dividends per share of $2.08 in the past year, up 5.6% from the previous year. This is the eighth consecutive dividend increase. In the past 8 years average annual compound growth rate of dividends was 6.1%.

3.2 Rule of 40

The stock scores a favorable score exceeding 40 when using the Rule of 40 (Revenue Growth plus EBITDA margin). Y.o.y revenue growth of 10%, EBITDA margin is 34.5% and the sum of the two 44.1% needs to exceed 40%.

Source: Alliant Energy Corporation's, 10-K filing dated February 20, 2026, available at the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission website: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/52485/000035254126000007/lnt-20251231.htm. company (Alliant Energy Corporation), Ticker(LNT)

3.3 Created Market Value [CMV] past 18 yrs of $11.8 billion

- Market Capitalization has increased by $14.4 billion from $4.2 billion to $18.6 billion in the last 18 years. This increase comprises cumulative retained earnings (RETE) of $2.6 billion and Created Market Value of $11.8 billion. The Created Market Value multiple, defined by the change in MCap for every $1 of retained earnings is exemplary at $5.58.

3.4 Satisfies 5 out of 9 criteria of Joseph Piotroski [pass mark 5]:

- Positive net income.

- Positive operating cashflow.

- Return on Assets improvement [from 3.0% to 3.2%].

- Good quality of earnings [operating cashflow exceeds net income].

- Improvement in current ratio from 0.4 to 0.8.

But does not meet the following 4 criteria of Joseph Piotroski:

- Improvement in long-term debt to total assets.

- Total shares on issue unchanged (or reduction in total shares on issue).

- Improvement in gross margin.

- Improvement in asset turnover.

Tue Apr 21, 2026: Alliant Energy Advances Bent Tree North Wind Project in Minnesota

Alliant Energy has announced that its Bent Tree North Wind project in Freeborn County, Minnesota, will proceed following recent regulatory approvals. The energy company received unanimous backing for the construction of a 150-megawatt (MW) project adjacent to its existing 200-MW Bent Tree wind farm. This approval was granted by both the Minnesota Public Utilities Commission (PUC) and the Public Service Commission of Wisconsin (PSC) on the 21st of April, 2026, marking a significant milestone for Alliant Energy.

Becky Valcq, president of Alliant Energy's Wisconsin energy company, emphasized that the unanimous approvals from regulators in both states reflect strong confidence in the project's potential benefits. "Approval of the Bent Tree North Wind project marks an important step as Alliant Energy continues to build a balanced energy portfolio that includes both renewable and traditional resources to meet customer demand," Valcq stated.

The Bent Tree North Wind project has garnered support from various stakeholders, including the Freeborn County Chamber of Commerce and multiple labor unions. These endorsements highlight the project's potential to stimulate economic growth in the region and enhance the reliability of the energy grid. Valerie Lockhart, executive director of the Albert Lea-Freeborn County Chamber of Commerce, noted, "The Bent Tree North Wind Project represents continued, meaningful investment in Freeborn County—creating jobs, supporting our tax base, and delivering long-term value to local landowners."

Alliant Energy projects that the new wind initiative will generate over $100 million in economic benefits, covering local tax revenue, payments to landowners, construction wages, and other community investments. The company's projections indicate that the project will produce sufficient renewable energy to power approximately 50,000 homes annually, reinforcing its commitment to sustainable energy solutions.

The Bent Tree North initiative is a key component of Alliant Energy's broader Energy Blueprint, which outlines the company's strategy for integrating renewable and traditional energy generation resources. This roadmap aims to provide cost-effective and reliable energy to customers while promoting a balanced energy future. For further details about the Bent Tree North Wind project and Alliant Energy's wind energy initiatives, visit the company's official website.

Recent News 2 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 315 words

Tue Apr 14, 2026: Alliant Energy's Strategic Moves Position it for Growth in a Shifting Economy

Alliant Energy Corporation is poised for resilient growth as industrial growth shifts toward data centers and infrastructure. This is particularly relevant for U.S. investors looking for defensive investments with built-in inflation protection. On 14th April 2026, Alliant Energy's regulated business model positions the company favorably to meet America's energy demands.

Alliant Energy Corporation, identified by the stock code US0188021085, provides a stake in a regulated utility that supplies electricity and natural gas primarily in Iowa and Wisconsin. Its regulated structure means that the company earns returns sanctioned by state regulatory commissions, shielding it from the economic fluctuations that often impact discretionary sectors more severely. For investors, this translates into a stable business model characterized by predictable cash flows derived from essential services rather than volatile commodity prices.

The utility generates power from a well-balanced mix of coal, natural gas, wind, and solar energy, and it continues to invest in renewable sources to meet environmental mandates. This diversification lessens the risks associated with fuel costs and positions Alliant for federal incentives tied to decarbonization efforts. Historically, U.S. utilities have offered dividends in the range of 3-4%, which adds to their appeal for long-term investors.

Shifts in industrial technology are creating favorable conditions for utilities such as Alliant. Sectors like data centers and infrastructure are becoming increasingly significant consumers of power. The global industrial tech market is projected to experience robust growth, particularly in automation and green technology, which are expected to grow at double-digit CAGRs. This rising demand for reliable baseload power corresponds well with Alliant's capabilities, particularly as hyperscale data centers expand.

Going forward, it will be important for investors to keep an eye on upcoming rate case outcomes and quarterly reports indicating load growth, as well as dividend announcements that reinforce income strategies. Relevant macroeconomic indicators, including Federal Reserve interest rate paths and infrastructure bill implementations, could either strengthen or weaken Alliant's market position.

Recent News 3 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 363 words

Tue Apr 14, 2026: Alliant Energy's New Strategy Focuses on Powering Data Centers in the Midwest

Alliant Energy Corporation has announced a strategic pivot aimed at addressing the growing electricity demands of hyperscale data centers in the Midwest. On the 13th of April 2026, the company revealed plans to leverage its regulated utility model to ensure grid reliability while targeting an ambitious annual earnings growth of 6% to 8%. This shift reflects a broader strategy to modernize the Midwest energy grid, aligning with the increasing demand for robust digital infrastructure.

Serving over 1 million electric and 400,000 natural gas customers through its subsidiaries, Interstate Power and Light and Wisconsin Power and Light, Alliant Energy is responding to a projected 6% compound annual growth rate in industrial technology through 2030. This strategic adaptation allows Alliant Energy to utilize its regulated utility framework—earning state-approved returns—to mitigate economic volatility while fulfilling the heavy baseload power requirements of modern data centers. By managing a balanced generation portfolio that includes wind, solar, natural gas, and coal, the company aims to ensure a sustainable energy future.

The utility's focus on grid modernization is designed to align with both federal decarbonization mandates and the technical needs of high-capacity data facilities. Analysts view this move as positioning Alliant Energy as a defensive investment, offering shareholders predictable dividends amid significant transitions in the energy market. The company's commitment to integrating digital infrastructure into its utility model reflects a proactive approach to the evolving energy landscape.

By prioritizing data center integration, Alliant Energy seeks to influence regional energy pricing and service reliability positively. Reports suggest that by aligning its investment strategies with long-term power needs, Alliant Energy intends to adopt a disciplined capital approach aimed at achieving its targeted annual earnings growth. This strategy not only mitigates interest rate sensitivity but also capitalizes on the increasing demand for reliable, large-scale power in the Upper Midwest.

The shift towards integrating data centers into its utility operations represents a pivotal recalibration for Alliant Energy, indicating that traditional power providers are now recognizing digital infrastructure as a key driver of grid modernization and long-term financial stability. As the landscape of energy generation continues to evolve, Alliant Energy's strategic focus on these high-growth sectors positions it favorably for future developments in the industry.

Alliant Energy Corporation (NASDAQ:LNT), the NASDAQ's 5th largest Utility company by market cap, increased 0.9% during its previous trading session to close at $74.06.

Tue Apr 21, 2026: Alliant Energy Advances Bent Tree North Wind Project in Minnesota

Alliant Energy has announced that its Bent Tree North Wind project in Freeborn County, Minnesota, will proceed following recent regulatory approvals. The energy company received unanimous backing for the construction of a 150-megawatt (MW) project adjacent to its existing 200-MW Bent Tree wind farm. This approval was granted by both the Minnesota Public Utilities Commission (PUC) and the Public Service Commission of Wisconsin (PSC) on the 21st of April, 2026, marking a significant milestone for Alliant Energy.

Becky Valcq, president of Alliant Energy's Wisconsin energy company, emphasized that the unanimous approvals from regulators in both states reflect strong confidence in the project's potential benefits. "Approval of the Bent Tree North Wind project marks an important step as Alliant Energy continues to build a balanced energy portfolio that includes both renewable and traditional resources to meet customer demand," Valcq stated.

The Bent Tree North Wind project has garnered support from various stakeholders, including the Freeborn County Chamber of Commerce and multiple labor unions. These endorsements highlight the project's potential to stimulate economic growth in the region and enhance the reliability of the energy grid. Valerie Lockhart, executive director of the Albert Lea-Freeborn County Chamber of Commerce, noted, "The Bent Tree North Wind Project represents continued, meaningful investment in Freeborn County—creating jobs, supporting our tax base, and delivering long-term value to local landowners."

Alliant Energy projects that the new wind initiative will generate over $100 million in economic benefits, covering local tax revenue, payments to landowners, construction wages, and other community investments. The company's projections indicate that the project will produce sufficient renewable energy to power approximately 50,000 homes annually, reinforcing its commitment to sustainable energy solutions.

The Bent Tree North initiative is a key component of Alliant Energy's broader Energy Blueprint, which outlines the company's strategy for integrating renewable and traditional energy generation resources. This roadmap aims to provide cost-effective and reliable energy to customers while promoting a balanced energy future. For further details about the Bent Tree North Wind project and Alliant Energy's wind energy initiatives, visit the company's official website.

Recent News 2 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 228 words

Tue Apr 14, 2026: Alliant Energy Corporation Sets Sights on Infrastructure Expansion Amid Rising Demand

Alliant Energy Corporation is aligning its regulated utility model to capture the rising demand from data centers and industrial infrastructure in Iowa and Wisconsin. As of 13th April 2026, the company aims to provide stable investor returns through its essential electricity and natural gas services.

The utility provider operates primarily through its subsidiaries, Interstate Power and Light Company and Wisconsin Power and Light Company. Alliant currently serves over 1 million electric and 400,000 natural gas customers across the Midwest region, highlighting its extensive footprint in a rapidly growing market.

Market data indicates that industrial technology growth is projected at a 6% compound annual growth rate through 2030. This expansion, largely driven by hyperscaler-led data center builds, is expected to significantly increase electricity needs within Alliant Energy's specific geographic footprint, creating opportunities for future revenue growth.

Alliant Energy maintains a diverse generation mix, which includes wind, solar, natural gas, and coal. This diversification helps reduce fuel cost risks while positioning the company to benefit from federal decarbonization incentives and grid-hardening programs, further enhancing its growth prospects.

Institutional analysts generally maintain hold to buy ratings on Alliant Energy stock, citing its track record of dividend increases and rate base growth. While interest rate sensitivity remains a factor for monitoring, the regulated model is viewed as a hedge against commodity price swings and broader market volatility, ensuring consistent returns for investors.

Recent News 3 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 315 words

Tue Apr 14, 2026: Alliant Energy's Strategic Moves Position it for Growth in a Shifting Economy

Alliant Energy Corporation is poised for resilient growth as industrial growth shifts toward data centers and infrastructure. This is particularly relevant for U.S. investors looking for defensive investments with built-in inflation protection. On 14th April 2026, Alliant Energy's regulated business model positions the company favorably to meet America's energy demands.

Alliant Energy Corporation, identified by the stock code US0188021085, provides a stake in a regulated utility that supplies electricity and natural gas primarily in Iowa and Wisconsin. Its regulated structure means that the company earns returns sanctioned by state regulatory commissions, shielding it from the economic fluctuations that often impact discretionary sectors more severely. For investors, this translates into a stable business model characterized by predictable cash flows derived from essential services rather than volatile commodity prices.

The utility generates power from a well-balanced mix of coal, natural gas, wind, and solar energy, and it continues to invest in renewable sources to meet environmental mandates. This diversification lessens the risks associated with fuel costs and positions Alliant for federal incentives tied to decarbonization efforts. Historically, U.S. utilities have offered dividends in the range of 3-4%, which adds to their appeal for long-term investors.

Shifts in industrial technology are creating favorable conditions for utilities such as Alliant. Sectors like data centers and infrastructure are becoming increasingly significant consumers of power. The global industrial tech market is projected to experience robust growth, particularly in automation and green technology, which are expected to grow at double-digit CAGRs. This rising demand for reliable baseload power corresponds well with Alliant's capabilities, particularly as hyperscale data centers expand.

Going forward, it will be important for investors to keep an eye on upcoming rate case outcomes and quarterly reports indicating load growth, as well as dividend announcements that reinforce income strategies. Relevant macroeconomic indicators, including Federal Reserve interest rate paths and infrastructure bill implementations, could either strengthen or weaken Alliant's market position.

SECTION 2 TODAY'S BULLISH SIGNALS

2.1 Other Bullish Signals

MCap/Total Assets:

- Tobin's Q Ratio, defined as MCap divided by Total Assets, is 0.8. Compared with the rest of the market the stock is undervalued.

- Net profit margin has averaged 18.3% in the last 3 years. This is considered superior and suggests a high margin of safety.

- As per the Du Pont analysis, Return on Equity of 11.0% is better than sector average of 10.7%. This is computed as net profit margin of 18.6% times asset turnover [sales/assets] of 0.17 times leverage factor [total assets/shareholders' equity] of 3.4.

SECTION 3 ONGOING BULLISH PARAMETERS

3.1 Alliant Energy Corporation sees dividend rise for an eighth consecutive year

Alliant Energy Corporation reported dividends per share of $2.08 in the past year, up 5.6% from the previous year. This is the eighth consecutive dividend increase. In the past 8 years average annual compound growth rate of dividends was 6.1%.

Source: Nasdaq Stock Exchange: https://www.nasdaq.com/market-activity/stocks/LNT/dividend-history, company (Alliant Energy Corporation), Ticker(LNT)

3.2 Rule of 40

The stock scores a favorable score exceeding 40 when using the Rule of 40 (Revenue Growth plus EBITDA margin). Y.o.y revenue growth of 10%, EBITDA margin is 34.5% and the sum of the two 44.1% needs to exceed 40%.

Source: Alliant Energy Corporation's, 10-K filing dated February 20, 2026, available at the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission website: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/52485/000035254126000007/lnt-20251231.htm. company (Alliant Energy Corporation), Ticker(LNT)

3.3 Past two-years

- The shares rose $11.49 (24.3%) a year ago which accelerated to $15.36 (26.2%) in the past year.

3.4 Created Market Value [CMV] past 18 yrs of $12.3 billion

- Market Capitalization has increased by $14.8 billion from $4.2 billion to $19 billion in the last 18 years. This increase comprises cumulative retained earnings (RETE) of $2.6 billion and Created Market Value of $12.3 billion. The Created Market Value multiple, defined by the change in MCap for every $1 of retained earnings is exemplary at $5.77.

3.5 Satisfies 5 out of 9 criteria of Joseph Piotroski [pass mark 5]:

- Positive net income.

- Positive operating cashflow.

- Return on Assets improvement [from 3.0% to 3.2%].

- Good quality of earnings [operating cashflow exceeds net income].

- Improvement in current ratio from 0.4 to 0.8.

But does not meet the following 4 criteria of Joseph Piotroski:

- Improvement in long-term debt to total assets.

- Total shares on issue unchanged (or reduction in total shares on issue).

- Improvement in gross margin.

- Improvement in asset turnover.

Source: Alliant Energy Corporation's, 10-K filing dated February 20, 2026, available at the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission website: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/52485/000035254126000007/lnt-20251231.htm. company (Alliant Energy Corporation), Ticker(LNT)

- Return on Capital Employed of 2.9% versus sector average of 4.5% and market average of 10.9%.

urn:contentItem:6JJP-2CB3-SHCH-60SN-00000-00Top Renewable Energy Stocks To Watch Now - May 10th2026-05-12T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:18:41ZLexisNexisTop Renewable Energy Stocks To Watch Now - May 10th

Quanta Services, WEC Energy Group, and HA Sustainable Infrastructure Capital are the three Renewable Energy stocks to watch today, according to MarketBeat's stock screener tool. Renewable energy stocks are shares of companies involved in producing, developing, or supporting energy sources that naturally replenish, such as solar, wind, hydroelectric, geothermal, and bioenergy. For stock market investors, these stocks offer a way to gain exposure to the clean energy sector, which may benefit from government policy, technological advances, and growing demand for sustainable power. These companies had the highest dollar trading volume of any Renewable Energy stocks within the last several days.

Quanta Services PWR

Quanta Services, Inc. provides infrastructure solutions for the electric and gas utility, renewable energy, communications, and pipeline and energy industries in the United States, Canada, Australia, and internationally. The company's Electric Power Infrastructure Solutions segment engages in the design, procurement, construction, upgrade, repair, and maintenance of electric power transmission and distribution infrastructure and substation facilities; installation, maintenance, and upgrade of electric power infrastructure projects; installation of smart grid technologies on electric power networks; and design, installation, maintenance, and repair of commercial and industrial wirings.

Read Our Latest Research Report on PWR

WEC Energy Group WEC

WEC Energy Group, Inc., through its subsidiaries, provides regulated natural gas and electricity, and renewable and nonregulated renewable energy services in the United States. It operates through Wisconsin, Illinois, Other States, Electric Transmission, and Non-Utility Energy Infrastructure segments.

Read Our Latest Research Report on WEC

HA Sustainable Infrastructure Capital HASI

HA Sustainable Infrastructure Capital, Inc., through its subsidiaries, engages in the investment of energy efficiency, renewable energy, and sustainable infrastructure markets in the United States. The company's portfolio includes equity investments, commercial and government receivables, real estate, and debt securities.

Read Our Latest Research Report on HASI

urn:contentItem:5Y78-NWP1-DXK2-M4NP-00000-00Top Renewable Energy Stocks To Watch Now - May 10th2026-05-10T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:18:41ZLexisNexisTop Renewable Energy Stocks To Watch Now - May 10th

Quanta Services, WEC Energy Group, and HA Sustainable Infrastructure Capital are the three Renewable Energy stocks to watch today, according to MarketBeat's stock screener tool. Renewable energy stocks are shares of companies involved in producing, developing, or supporting energy sources that naturally replenish, such as solar, wind, hydroelectric, geothermal, and bioenergy. For stock market investors, these stocks offer a way to gain exposure to the clean energy sector, which may benefit from government policy, technological advances, and growing demand for sustainable power. These companies had the highest dollar trading volume of any Renewable Energy stocks within the last several days.

Quanta Services PWR

Quanta Services, Inc. provides infrastructure solutions for the electric and gas utility, renewable energy, communications, and pipeline and energy industries in the United States, Canada, Australia, and internationally. The company's Electric Power Infrastructure Solutions segment engages in the design, procurement, construction, upgrade, repair, and maintenance of electric power transmission and distribution infrastructure and substation facilities; installation, maintenance, and upgrade of electric power infrastructure projects; installation of smart grid technologies on electric power networks; and design, installation, maintenance, and repair of commercial and industrial wirings.

Read Our Latest Research Report on PWR

WEC Energy Group WEC

WEC Energy Group, Inc., through its subsidiaries, provides regulated natural gas and electricity, and renewable and nonregulated renewable energy services in the United States. It operates through Wisconsin, Illinois, Other States, Electric Transmission, and Non-Utility Energy Infrastructure segments.

Read Our Latest Research Report on WEC

HA Sustainable Infrastructure Capital HASI

HA Sustainable Infrastructure Capital, Inc., through its subsidiaries, engages in the investment of energy efficiency, renewable energy, and sustainable infrastructure markets in the United States. The company's portfolio includes equity investments, commercial and government receivables, real estate, and debt securities.

Read Our Latest Research Report on HASI

Newstex and its re-distributors expressly reserve the right to delete stories at its and their sole discretion.

Kyle Jackson

May 9th, 2026 ( Watchlist News - Delivered by Newstex )

Quanta Services, WEC Energy Group, and HA Sustainable Infrastructure Capital are the three Renewable Energy stocks to watch today, according to MarketBeat's stock screener tool. Renewable energy stocks are shares of companies involved in producing, developing, or supporting energy sources that naturally replenish, such as solar, wind, hydroelectric, geothermal, and bioenergy. For stock market investors, these stocks offer a way to gain exposure to the clean energy sector, which may benefit from government policy, technological advances, and growing demand for sustainable power. These companies had the highest dollar trading volume of any Renewable Energy stocks within the last several days.

Quanta Services (PWR)

Quanta Services, Inc. provides infrastructure solutions for the electric and gas utility, renewable energy, communications, and pipeline and energy industries in the United States, Canada, Australia, and internationally. The company's Electric Power Infrastructure Solutions segment engages in the design, procurement, construction, upgrade, repair, and maintenance of electric power transmission and distribution infrastructure and substation facilities; installation, maintenance, and upgrade of electric power infrastructure projects; installation of smart grid technologies on electric power networks; and design, installation, maintenance, and repair of commercial and industrial wirings.

Read Our Latest Research Report on PWR

WEC Energy Group (WEC)

WEC Energy Group, Inc., through its subsidiaries, provides regulated natural gas and electricity, and renewable and nonregulated renewable energy services in the United States. It operates through Wisconsin, Illinois, Other States, Electric Transmission, and Non-Utility Energy Infrastructure segments.

Read Our Latest Research Report on WEC

HA Sustainable Infrastructure Capital (HASI)

HA Sustainable Infrastructure Capital, Inc., through its subsidiaries, engages in the investment of energy efficiency, renewable energy, and sustainable infrastructure markets in the United States. The company's portfolio includes equity investments, commercial and government receivables, real estate, and debt securities.

Read Our Latest Research Report on HASI

The Nasdaq's Historic Rally Doesn't Mean the Risk Is Gone

Quanta Services, WEC Energy Group, and HA Sustainable Infrastructure Capital are the three Renewable Energy stocks to watch today, according to MarketBeat's stock screener tool. Renewable energy stocks are shares of publicly traded companies that generate power from, build equipment for, or provide services to technologies using renewable resources such as solar, wind, hydro, geothermal, and biomass. For investors, they offer exposure to the clean-energy transition across utilities, equipment manufacturers, project developers and financing vehicles, but their returns can be influenced by government policy, technology shifts and project-level or commodity risks. These companies had the highest dollar trading volume of any Renewable Energy stocks within the last several days.

Quanta Services PWR

Quanta Services, Inc. provides infrastructure solutions for the electric and gas utility, renewable energy, communications, and pipeline and energy industries in the United States, Canada, Australia, and internationally. The company's Electric Power Infrastructure Solutions segment engages in the design, procurement, construction, upgrade, repair, and maintenance of electric power transmission and distribution infrastructure and substation facilities; installation, maintenance, and upgrade of electric power infrastructure projects; installation of smart grid technologies on electric power networks; and design, installation, maintenance, and repair of commercial and industrial wirings.

The Power Bill, the AI Dip, and the Date That Could Flip 2026 Stocks

WEC Energy Group WEC

WEC Energy Group, Inc., through its subsidiaries, provides regulated natural gas and electricity, and renewable and nonregulated renewable energy services in the United States. It operates through Wisconsin, Illinois, Other States, Electric Transmission, and Non-Utility Energy Infrastructure segments.

HA Sustainable Infrastructure Capital HASI

HA Sustainable Infrastructure Capital, Inc., through its subsidiaries, engages in the investment of energy efficiency, renewable energy, and sustainable infrastructure markets in the United States. The company's portfolio includes equity investments, commercial and government receivables, real estate, and debt securities.
