May 7th, 2026 ( Zolmax.com - Delivered by Newstex )

Quanta Services, WEC Energy Group, and HA Sustainable Infrastructure Capital are the three Renewable Energy stocks to watch today, according to MarketBeat's stock screener tool. Renewable energy stocks are shares of publicly traded companies that generate power from, build equipment for, or provide services to technologies using renewable resources such as solar, wind, hydro, geothermal, and biomass. For investors, they offer exposure to the clean-energy transition across utilities, equipment manufacturers, project developers and financing vehicles, but their returns can be influenced by government policy, technology shifts and project-level or commodity risks. These companies had the highest dollar trading volume of any Renewable Energy stocks within the last several days.

Quanta Services (PWR)

Quanta Services, Inc. provides infrastructure solutions for the electric and gas utility, renewable energy, communications, and pipeline and energy industries in the United States, Canada, Australia, and internationally. The company's Electric Power Infrastructure Solutions segment engages in the design, procurement, construction, upgrade, repair, and maintenance of electric power transmission and distribution infrastructure and substation facilities; installation, maintenance, and upgrade of electric power infrastructure projects; installation of smart grid technologies on electric power networks; and design, installation, maintenance, and repair of commercial and industrial wirings.

Read Our Latest Research Report on PWR

WEC Energy Group (WEC)

WEC Energy Group, Inc., through its subsidiaries, provides regulated natural gas and electricity, and renewable and nonregulated renewable energy services in the United States. It operates through Wisconsin, Illinois, Other States, Electric Transmission, and Non-Utility Energy Infrastructure segments.

Read Our Latest Research Report on WEC

HA Sustainable Infrastructure Capital (HASI)

HA Sustainable Infrastructure Capital, Inc., through its subsidiaries, engages in the investment of energy efficiency, renewable energy, and sustainable infrastructure markets in the United States. The company's portfolio includes equity investments, commercial and government receivables, real estate, and debt securities.

Read Our Latest Research Report on HASI

Link to the original story.

May 7th, 2026 ( DailyPolitical - Delivered by Newstex )

Quanta Services, WEC Energy Group, and HA Sustainable Infrastructure Capital are the three Renewable Energy stocks to watch today, according to MarketBeat's stock screener tool. Renewable energy stocks are shares of publicly traded companies that generate power from, build equipment for, or provide services to technologies using renewable resources such as solar, wind, hydro, geothermal, and biomass. For investors, they offer exposure to the clean-energy transition across utilities, equipment manufacturers, project developers and financing vehicles, but their returns can be influenced by government policy, technology shifts and project-level or commodity risks. These companies had the highest dollar trading volume of any Renewable Energy stocks within the last several days.

Quanta Services (PWR)

Quanta Services, Inc. provides infrastructure solutions for the electric and gas utility, renewable energy, communications, and pipeline and energy industries in the United States, Canada, Australia, and internationally. The company's Electric Power Infrastructure Solutions segment engages in the design, procurement, construction, upgrade, repair, and maintenance of electric power transmission and distribution infrastructure and substation facilities; installation, maintenance, and upgrade of electric power infrastructure projects; installation of smart grid technologies on electric power networks; and design, installation, maintenance, and repair of commercial and industrial wirings.

Read Our Latest Research Report on PWR

WEC Energy Group (WEC)

WEC Energy Group, Inc., through its subsidiaries, provides regulated natural gas and electricity, and renewable and nonregulated renewable energy services in the United States. It operates through Wisconsin, Illinois, Other States, Electric Transmission, and Non-Utility Energy Infrastructure segments.

Read Our Latest Research Report on WEC

HA Sustainable Infrastructure Capital (HASI)

HA Sustainable Infrastructure Capital, Inc., through its subsidiaries, engages in the investment of energy efficiency, renewable energy, and sustainable infrastructure markets in the United States. The company's portfolio includes equity investments, commercial and government receivables, real estate, and debt securities.

Read Our Latest Research Report on HASI

Quanta Services, WEC Energy Group, and NOV are the three Renewable Energy stocks to watch today, according to MarketBeat's stock screener tool. Renewable energy stocks are shares of companies involved in generating, developing, manufacturing equipment for, or providing services to energy sources that are naturally replenishedsuch as solar, wind, hydro, geothermal, and biomass. For investors, these equities offer a way to gain exposure to the clean-energy transition and tend to behave as growth- or policy-sensitive investments influenced by technology, regulation, and commodity or project risks. These companies had the highest dollar trading volume of any Renewable Energy stocks within the last several days.

Quanta Services (PWR)

Quanta Services, Inc. provides infrastructure solutions for the electric and gas utility, renewable energy, communications, and pipeline and energy industries in the United States, Canada, Australia, and internationally. The company's Electric Power Infrastructure Solutions segment engages in the design, procurement, construction, upgrade, repair, and maintenance of electric power transmission and distribution infrastructure and substation facilities; installation, maintenance, and upgrade of electric power infrastructure projects; installation of smart grid technologies on electric power networks; and design, installation, maintenance, and repair of commercial and industrial wirings.

WEC Energy Group (WEC)

WEC Energy Group, Inc., through its subsidiaries, provides regulated natural gas and electricity, and renewable and nonregulated renewable energy services in the United States. It operates through Wisconsin, Illinois, Other States, Electric Transmission, and Non-Utility Energy Infrastructure segments.

Read Our Latest Research Report on WEC

NOV (NOV)

NOV Inc. designs, constructs, manufactures, and sells systems, components, and products for oil and gas drilling and production, and industrial and renewable energy sectors in the United States and internationally. It operates through two segments, Energy Equipment, and Energy Products and Services. The company provides solids control and waste management equipment and services, managed pressure drilling, drilling fluids, premium drillpipe, wired pipe, drilling optimization services, tubular inspection and coating services, instrumentation, downhole tools, and drill bits.

Newstex and its re-distributors expressly reserve the right to delete stories at its and their sole discretion.

Kyle Jackson

May 3rd, 2026 ( Watchlist News - Delivered by Newstex )

Quanta Services, WEC Energy Group, and NOV are the three Renewable Energy stocks to watch today, according to MarketBeat's stock screener tool. Renewable energy stocks are shares of companies whose primary business is generating, manufacturing equipment for, developing projects in, or providing services and technologies related to clean energy sources (for example solar, wind, hydro, geothermal, biomass, and energy storage). Investors buy these equities to gain exposure to the transition away from fossil fuels and potential long-term growth, while accepting risks tied to policy changes, subsidies, technology shifts, and project or commodity cycles. These companies had the highest dollar trading volume of any Renewable Energy stocks within the last several days.

Quanta Services (PWR)

Quanta Services, Inc. provides infrastructure solutions for the electric and gas utility, renewable energy, communications, and pipeline and energy industries in the United States, Canada, Australia, and internationally. The company's Electric Power Infrastructure Solutions segment engages in the design, procurement, construction, upgrade, repair, and maintenance of electric power transmission and distribution infrastructure and substation facilities; installation, maintenance, and upgrade of electric power infrastructure projects; installation of smart grid technologies on electric power networks; and design, installation, maintenance, and repair of commercial and industrial wirings.

Read Our Latest Research Report on PWR

WEC Energy Group (WEC)

WEC Energy Group, Inc., through its subsidiaries, provides regulated natural gas and electricity, and renewable and nonregulated renewable energy services in the United States. It operates through Wisconsin, Illinois, Other States, Electric Transmission, and Non-Utility Energy Infrastructure segments.

Read Our Latest Research Report on WEC

NOV (NOV)

NOV Inc. designs, constructs, manufactures, and sells systems, components, and products for oil and gas drilling and production, and industrial and renewable energy sectors in the United States and internationally. It operates through two segments, Energy Equipment, and Energy Products and Services. The company provides solids control and waste management equipment and services, managed pressure drilling, drilling fluids, premium drillpipe, wired pipe, drilling optimization services, tubular inspection and coating services, instrumentation, downhole tools, and drill bits.

Read Our Latest Research Report on NOV

value.Title: WEC Energy Group 10-Q: Strategic Debt Issuance to Fund Growth Amid Rising Costs and 11 Other Management Decisions

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JGB-8C73-RSVS-J4K3-00000-00WEC Energy Group 10-Q: Strategic Debt Issuance to Fund Growth Amid Rising Costs and 11 Other Management Decisions2026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:18:41ZLexisNexisWEC Energy Group 10-Q: Strategic Debt Issuance to Fund Growth Amid Rising Costs and 11 Other Management Decisions

NYSE STOCK REPORT

To finance its capital plan and operations, management issued long-term debt throughout 2024 and 2025. While necessary to maintain liquidity and execute growth projects, this action raised interest expense by $49 million in the corporate segment. Investors must balance the necessity of this capital access against the impact on coverage ratios and credit ratings. Management's ability to balance maturity schedules with investment needs remains central to sustaining financial discipline in a higher-rate environment.

Aggressive Capital Deployment in Regulated Assets to Drive Rate Base Expansion

Management authorized a robust $37.5 billion capital plan spanning 2026-2030, heavily weighted toward regulated utilities and ATC investments. This strategic allocation is designed to bolster infrastructure reliability and facilitate the clean energy transition while targeting long-term earnings growth through regulated returns. While investors stand to gain from projected rate base expansion and dividend stability, execution risks-specifically regarding regulatory approvals and potential cost overruns-remain critical factors to watch as the company scales its operations to meet rising energy demand.

Wisconsin Renewable Investments to Leverage Tax Credits and ESG Upside

A focused $12.6 billion investment in Wisconsin-based regulated renewable energy is planned for 2026-2030, delivering 3,850 MWs of solar, 2,130 MWs of battery storage, and 555 MWs of wind. This portfolio shift aligns with decarbonization goals and regulatory mandates, positioning the company to capitalize on IRA and OBBBA tax credits. Beyond direct financial incentives, this move improves ESG profiles and reduces carbon risk exposure, supporting net carbon neutrality goals by 2050. Investors should note that while rate recovery mechanisms support earnings visibility, supply chain constraints could impact project timelines.

Strategic Natural Gas Expansion to Ensure Reliability Amid Demand Surges

To address load growth from data centers and manufacturing, management is allocating $5.4 billion to natural gas-fired generation between 2026-2030, adding 3,300 MWs of CTs and 180 MWs of RICE units. This investment diversifies the generation mix, offering a reliable hedge against renewable intermittency and facilitating the retirement of older fossil fuel assets. While this strategy stabilizes revenue and reduces environmental liabilities, returns remain sensitive to natural gas price volatility and regulatory scrutiny regarding long-term fossil fuel reliance.

Decarbonization Roadmap: Accelerating Coal Retirements for Risk Mitigation

Management has committed to a net carbon neutral electric generation goal by 2050, strategically phasing out coal as a primary fuel source by 2030 and eliminating it entirely by 2032. This operational pivot involves significant capital investment but directly mitigates regulatory and reputational risks associated with high-carbon assets. For investors, this transition may enhance valuation multiples by attracting ESG-focused capital. However, the costs associated with transition expenses and asset retirements could pressure near-term earnings despite long-term operational efficiencies.

Illinois Regulatory Settlement to Clear Uncertainty and Stabilize Outlook

A $205 million settlement with the Illinois Attorney General regarding UEA and QIP rider proceedings resolves significant legal overhang. While the resulting charge impacts 2025 earnings, this decision eliminates the risk of prolonged litigation and potentially larger future disallowances. By clearing these regulatory hurdles, management provides investors with a more predictable financial outlook for Illinois operations, allowing a renewed focus on core operational execution and dividend stability.

Portfolio Modernization Through Fossil Fuel Retirements and Compliance

Since 2018, management has retired nearly 2,500 MWs of fossil-fueled generation, with an additional 900 MWs slated for retirement by 2031. These moves ensure compliance with EPA regulations and reduce long-term environmental compliance costs. Although write-offs may cause temporary earnings dips, replacing these assets with renewables and modern gas facilities improves overall asset efficiency. This modernization strategy is critical for maintaining regulatory favorability and positioning the portfolio for sustainable growth.

Targeted Tariffs to Capture Data Center Load Growth

Management filed proposed VLC and Bespoke Resources tariffs with the PSCW in 2025 to specifically address the unique needs of large customers, such as data centers. This strategic pricing mechanism aims to capture up to 6.1 GWs of potential load growth through 2030 without subsidizing costs across the broader customer base. Approval of these tariffs would signal regulatory adaptability and unlock a significant revenue stream for investors, validating the company's ability to monetize economic development trends.

Grid Resilience Investment Through Large-Scale Battery Storage

A $2.9 billion allocation for Battery Energy Storage Systems (BESS) is planned for 2026-2030 to fortify grid reliability. This investment enables the capture of excess renewable power for peak demand usage, integrating clean energy assets more effectively. For investors, this enhances operational resilience against weather-related disruptions and stabilizes earnings through rate recoveries. As a high-growth area aligned with clean energy trends, this deployment supports valuation expansion by modernizing grid capabilities.

Non-Utility Renewable Acquisitions to Boost Tax-Advantaged Earnings

Management aggressively expanded its non-utility infrastructure by acquiring additional renewable facilities in late 2024 and early 2025. These acquisitions are projected to drive a $30.3 million earnings increase in 2025, primarily through Production Tax Credits (PTCs). This strategy diversifies revenue streams beyond regulated utilities and provides tax-advantaged income. While increased debt service is a factor, the accretive nature of these assets supports overall EPS growth and leverages federal incentives effectively.

Escrow Accounting Implementation to Smooth Earnings Volatility

In compliance with PSCW orders, management implemented escrow accounting for pension and OPEB costs at its Wisconsin utilities. This accounting treatment defers cost variances, effectively smoothing out earnings volatility related to market asset returns. For investors, this results in more predictable financial reporting and valuation stability. By mitigating the immediate impact of market fluctuations, this mechanism helps preserve dividend capacity and financial discipline.

Infrastructure Safety Investments: Eliminating Cast Iron Risk

Management is executing a long-term plan to retire cast and ductile iron pipes by 2035 via the PRP, aligning with ICC directives. These investments enhance safety and reliability within the Illinois infrastructure network, with costs recoverable through rate cases. This proactive approach reduces the risk of catastrophic failures and associated penalties, protecting long-term shareholder value. Regulatory oversight ensures capital is deployed prudently, securing returns on these essential safety upgrades.

May 3rd, 2026 ( Zolmax.com - Delivered by Newstex )

MGE Energy ( NASDAQ:MGEE - Get Free Report) is anticipated to issue its Q1 2026 results before the market opens on Wednesday, May 6th. Analysts expect the company to announce earnings of $1.13 per share and revenue of $227.06 million for the quarter. Investors can find conference call details on the company's upcoming Q1 2026 earning report page for the latest details on the call scheduled for Wednesday, May 13, 2026 at 12:30 PM ET.

MGE Energy ( NASDAQ:MGEE - Get Free Report) last issued its earnings results on Tuesday, February 24th. The utilities provider reported $0.64 EPS for the quarter, meeting analysts' consensus estimates of $0.64. MGE Energy had a return on equity of 10.60% and a net margin of 18.27%.The company had revenue of $189.55 million during the quarter. On average, analysts expect MGE Energy to post $4 EPS for the current fiscal year and $4 EPS for the next fiscal year.

MGE Energy Stock Performance

Shares of MGEE stock opened at $80.60 on Monday. The company has a quick ratio of 0.55, a current ratio of 0.77 and a debt-to-equity ratio of 0.61. The business has a 50 day moving average of $78.47 and a two-hundred day moving average of $80.10. MGE Energy has a 52 week low of $72.17 and a 52 week high of $94.00. The company has a market cap of $2.96 billion, a P/E ratio of 21.67 and a beta of 0.76.

Analyst Upgrades and Downgrades

A number of equities analysts have commented on MGEE shares. Wall Street Zen upgraded MGE Energy from a "sell" rating to a "hold" rating in a research note on Sunday, March 8th. Morgan Stanley restated an "underweight" rating on shares of MGE Energy in a research note on Monday, April 27th. Two analysts have rated the stock with a Hold rating and one has assigned a Sell rating to the stock. According to MarketBeat, the stock currently has an average rating of "Reduce" and a consensus target price of $80.50.

View Our Latest Research Report on MGE Energy

Insider Buying and Selling

In other news, Director James G. Berbee bought 373 shares of the company's stock in a transaction that occurred on Wednesday, March 11th. The shares were purchased at an average price of $77.74 per share, with a total value of $28,997.02. Following the completion of the acquisition, the director directly owned 9,362 shares in the company, valued at approximately $727,801.88. This trade represents a 4.15% increase in their ownership of the stock. The purchase was disclosed in a legal filing with the Securities & Exchange Commission, which is available through the SEC website. 0.41% of the stock is currently owned by insiders.

Institutional Investors Weigh In On MGE Energy

Several hedge funds and other institutional investors have recently added to or reduced their stakes in MGEE. CIBC Bancorp USA Inc. bought a new position in shares of MGE Energy in the third quarter valued at $331,000. Schonfeld Strategic Advisors LLC bought a new stake in shares of MGE Energy during the 3rd quarter worth $327,000. Counterpoint Mutual Funds LLC bought a new stake in shares of MGE Energy during the 4th quarter worth $288,000. Guggenheim Capital LLC acquired a new position in shares of MGE Energy in the 4th quarter worth $268,000. Finally, Beacon Pointe Advisors LLC grew its holdings in shares of MGE Energy by 6.6% in the 4th quarter. Beacon Pointe Advisors LLC now owns 2,826 shares of the utilities provider's stock worth $222,000 after acquiring an additional 174 shares during the last quarter. Institutional investors own 52.58% of the company's stock.

MGE Energy Company Profile

( Get Free Report)

MGE Energy, Inc is a public utility holding company headquartered in Madison, Wisconsin. Through its principal subsidiary, Madison Gas and Electric Company (MGE), the company provides regulated electric and natural gas distribution services to residential, commercial and industrial customers in south-central Wisconsin. MGE Energy's operations encompass the generation, transmission and distribution of electricity as well as the purchase and distribution of natural gas, with an emphasis on reliable service and system resilience.

Since reorganizing as a holding company in 2001, MGE Energy has expanded its portfolio to include nonregulated subsidiaries involved in renewable energy development, energy services and infrastructure support.

May 3rd, 2026 ( Watchlist News - Delivered by Newstex )

Brookfield Renewable Partners ( NYSE:BEP - Get Free Report) (TSE:BEP) had its price target hoisted by research analysts at TD from $38.00 to $39.00 in a research report issued on Monday, BayStreet.CA reports. The brokerage currently has a "buy" rating on the utilities provider's stock. TD's price target would suggest a potential upside of 18.51% from the stock's current price.

Other equities analysts have also issued research reports about the company. JPMorgan Chase & Co. raised their price objective on Brookfield Renewable Partners from $34.00 to $40.00 and gave the stock an "overweight" rating in a research note on Thursday, April 16th. Weiss Ratings restated a "hold (c-)" rating on shares of Brookfield Renewable Partners in a research note on Friday, March 27th. Canadian Imperial Bank of Commerce restated an "outperform" rating and set a $40.00 price objective (up from $37.00) on shares of Brookfield Renewable Partners in a research note on Monday, April 20th. Morgan Stanley lifted their price target on Brookfield Renewable Partners from $38.00 to $42.00 and gave the company an "overweight" rating in a research note on Monday, March 23rd. Finally, TD Securities reiterated a "buy" rating and issued a $39.00 price target on shares of Brookfield Renewable Partners in a research note on Monday. One research analyst has rated the stock with a Strong Buy rating, twelve have given a Buy rating and four have given a Hold rating to the company. According to MarketBeat, the stock currently has a consensus rating of "Moderate Buy" and a consensus target price of $36.53.

Check Out Our Latest Research Report on BEP

Brookfield Renewable Partners Price Performance

Brookfield Renewable Partners stock traded down $0.44 during mid-day trading on Monday, reaching $32.91. 703,604 shares of the company were exchanged, compared to its average volume of 747,495. The company has a fifty day simple moving average of $32.40 and a 200-day simple moving average of $30.10. Brookfield Renewable Partners has a 12 month low of $22.17 and a 12 month high of $35.97. The company has a debt-to-equity ratio of 0.80, a current ratio of 0.57 and a quick ratio of 0.57. The firm has a market cap of $9.98 billion, a PE ratio of -106.13 and a beta of 1.06.

Brookfield Renewable Partners ( NYSE:BEP - Get Free Report) (TSE:BEP) last posted its quarterly earnings data on Friday, May 1st. The utilities provider reported ($0.40) EPS for the quarter, missing analysts' consensus estimates of ($0.36) by ($0.04). Brookfield Renewable Partners had a return on equity of 1.59% and a net margin of 8.28%.The company had revenue of $1.51 billion for the quarter, compared to the consensus estimate of $1.50 billion. As a group, equities research analysts expect that Brookfield Renewable Partners will post -1.18 earnings per share for the current fiscal year.

Institutional Investors Weigh In On Brookfield Renewable Partners

Several hedge funds have recently modified their holdings of the company. BROOKFIELD Corp ON boosted its holdings in shares of Brookfield Renewable Partners by 9.4% during the 4th quarter. BROOKFIELD Corp ON now owns 81,306,719 shares of the utilities provider's stock valued at $2,192,842,000 after acquiring an additional 6,967,670 shares in the last quarter. Principal Financial Group Inc. boosted its holdings in shares of Brookfield Renewable Partners by 1.8% during the 4th quarter. Principal Financial Group Inc. now owns 10,305,148 shares of the utilities provider's stock valued at $277,930,000 after acquiring an additional 177,599 shares in the last quarter. Bank of Montreal Can boosted its holdings in shares of Brookfield Renewable Partners by 20.3% during the 4th quarter. Bank of Montreal Can now owns 8,394,947 shares of the utilities provider's stock valued at $226,859,000 after acquiring an additional 1,415,989 shares in the last quarter. State of Wisconsin Investment Board boosted its holdings in shares of Brookfield Renewable Partners by 128.0% during the 4th quarter. State of Wisconsin Investment Board now owns 4,867,084 shares of the utilities provider's stock valued at $131,265,000 after acquiring an additional 2,732,542 shares in the last quarter. Finally, Morgan Stanley boosted its holdings in shares of Brookfield Renewable Partners by 41.9% during the 4th quarter. Morgan Stanley now owns 4,029,046 shares of the utilities provider's stock valued at $108,663,000 after acquiring an additional 1,189,130 shares in the last quarter. Institutional investors and hedge funds own 63.16% of the company's stock.

Brookfield Renewable Partners Company Profile

( Get Free Report)

Brookfield Renewable Partners L.P. is a leading global owner, operator and developer of renewable power assets. Listed on the New York Stock Exchange under the ticker BEP, the partnership focuses on generating clean electricity from a diversified mix of hydroelectric, wind, solar and energy storage facilities. As part of the Brookfield Asset Management group, Brookfield Renewable leverages a long-term, asset-backed approach to investing in sustainable energy projects that support the transition to a low-carbon economy.

The company's platform encompasses approximately 23,000 megawatts of installed capacity across four continents.

urn:contentItem:6JHY-SWR3-S7VP-F0BV-00000-00Renewable Energy Stocks To Keep An Eye On - May 5th2026-05-08T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:18:41ZLexisNexisRenewable Energy Stocks To Keep An Eye On - May 5th

Quanta Services, WEC Energy Group, and NOV are the three Renewable Energy stocks to watch today, according to MarketBeat's stock screener tool. Renewable energy stocks are shares of companies involved in generating, developing, manufacturing equipment for, or providing services to energy sources that are naturally replenished-such as solar, wind, hydro, geothermal, and biomass. For investors, these equities offer a way to gain exposure to the clean-energy transition and tend to behave as growth- or policy-sensitive investments influenced by technology, regulation, and commodity or project risks. These companies had the highest dollar trading volume of any Renewable Energy stocks within the last several days.

Quanta Services PWR

Quanta Services, Inc. provides infrastructure solutions for the electric and gas utility, renewable energy, communications, and pipeline and energy industries in the United States, Canada, Australia, and internationally. The company's Electric Power Infrastructure Solutions segment engages in the design, procurement, construction, upgrade, repair, and maintenance of electric power transmission and distribution infrastructure and substation facilities; installation, maintenance, and upgrade of electric power infrastructure projects; installation of smart grid technologies on electric power networks; and design, installation, maintenance, and repair of commercial and industrial wirings.

Read Our Latest Research Report on PWR

WEC Energy Group WEC

WEC Energy Group, Inc., through its subsidiaries, provides regulated natural gas and electricity, and renewable and nonregulated renewable energy services in the United States. It operates through Wisconsin, Illinois, Other States, Electric Transmission, and Non-Utility Energy Infrastructure segments.

Read Our Latest Research Report on WEC

NOV NOV

NOV Inc. designs, constructs, manufactures, and sells systems, components, and products for oil and gas drilling and production, and industrial and renewable energy sectors in the United States and internationally. It operates through two segments, Energy Equipment, and Energy Products and Services. The company provides solids control and waste management equipment and services, managed pressure drilling, drilling fluids, premium drillpipe, wired pipe, drilling optimization services, tubular inspection and coating services, instrumentation, downhole tools, and drill bits.

Read Our Latest Research Report on NOV

Tue Apr 21, 2026: Alliant Energy Advances Bent Tree North Wind Project in Minnesota

Alliant Energy has announced that its Bent Tree North Wind project in Freeborn County, Minnesota, will proceed following recent regulatory approvals. The energy company received unanimous backing for the construction of a 150-megawatt (MW) project adjacent to its existing 200-MW Bent Tree wind farm. This approval was granted by both the Minnesota Public Utilities Commission (PUC) and the Public Service Commission of Wisconsin (PSC) on the 21st of April, 2026, marking a significant milestone for Alliant Energy.

Becky Valcq, president of Alliant Energy's Wisconsin energy company, emphasized that the unanimous approvals from regulators in both states reflect strong confidence in the project's potential benefits. "Approval of the Bent Tree North Wind project marks an important step as Alliant Energy continues to build a balanced energy portfolio that includes both renewable and traditional resources to meet customer demand," Valcq stated.

The Bent Tree North Wind project has garnered support from various stakeholders, including the Freeborn County Chamber of Commerce and multiple labor unions. These endorsements highlight the project's potential to stimulate economic growth in the region and enhance the reliability of the energy grid. Valerie Lockhart, executive director of the Albert Lea-Freeborn County Chamber of Commerce, noted, "The Bent Tree North Wind Project represents continued, meaningful investment in Freeborn County—creating jobs, supporting our tax base, and delivering long-term value to local landowners."

Alliant Energy projects that the new wind initiative will generate over $100 million in economic benefits, covering local tax revenue, payments to landowners, construction wages, and other community investments. The company's projections indicate that the project will produce sufficient renewable energy to power approximately 50,000 homes annually, reinforcing its commitment to sustainable energy solutions.

The Bent Tree North initiative is a key component of Alliant Energy's broader Energy Blueprint, which outlines the company's strategy for integrating renewable and traditional energy generation resources. This roadmap aims to provide cost-effective and reliable energy to customers while promoting a balanced energy future. For further details about the Bent Tree North Wind project and Alliant Energy's wind energy initiatives, visit the company's official website.

Recent News 2 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 228 words

Tue Apr 14, 2026: Alliant Energy Corporation Sets Sights on Infrastructure Expansion Amid Rising Demand

Alliant Energy Corporation is aligning its regulated utility model to capture the rising demand from data centers and industrial infrastructure in Iowa and Wisconsin. As of 13th April 2026, the company aims to provide stable investor returns through its essential electricity and natural gas services.

The utility provider operates primarily through its subsidiaries, Interstate Power and Light Company and Wisconsin Power and Light Company. Alliant currently serves over 1 million electric and 400,000 natural gas customers across the Midwest region, highlighting its extensive footprint in a rapidly growing market.

Market data indicates that industrial technology growth is projected at a 6% compound annual growth rate through 2030. This expansion, largely driven by hyperscaler-led data center builds, is expected to significantly increase electricity needs within Alliant Energy's specific geographic footprint, creating opportunities for future revenue growth.

Alliant Energy maintains a diverse generation mix, which includes wind, solar, natural gas, and coal. This diversification helps reduce fuel cost risks while positioning the company to benefit from federal decarbonization incentives and grid-hardening programs, further enhancing its growth prospects.

Institutional analysts generally maintain hold to buy ratings on Alliant Energy stock, citing its track record of dividend increases and rate base growth. While interest rate sensitivity remains a factor for monitoring, the regulated model is viewed as a hedge against commodity price swings and broader market volatility, ensuring consistent returns for investors.

Recent News 3 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 363 words

Tue Apr 14, 2026: Alliant Energy's New Strategy Focuses on Powering Data Centers in the Midwest

Alliant Energy Corporation has announced a strategic pivot aimed at addressing the growing electricity demands of hyperscale data centers in the Midwest. On the 13th of April 2026, the company revealed plans to leverage its regulated utility model to ensure grid reliability while targeting an ambitious annual earnings growth of 6% to 8%. This shift reflects a broader strategy to modernize the Midwest energy grid, aligning with the increasing demand for robust digital infrastructure.

Serving over 1 million electric and 400,000 natural gas customers through its subsidiaries, Interstate Power and Light and Wisconsin Power and Light, Alliant Energy is responding to a projected 6% compound annual growth rate in industrial technology through 2030. This strategic adaptation allows Alliant Energy to utilize its regulated utility framework—earning state-approved returns—to mitigate economic volatility while fulfilling the heavy baseload power requirements of modern data centers. By managing a balanced generation portfolio that includes wind, solar, natural gas, and coal, the company aims to ensure a sustainable energy future.

The utility's focus on grid modernization is designed to align with both federal decarbonization mandates and the technical needs of high-capacity data facilities. Analysts view this move as positioning Alliant Energy as a defensive investment, offering shareholders predictable dividends amid significant transitions in the energy market. The company's commitment to integrating digital infrastructure into its utility model reflects a proactive approach to the evolving energy landscape.

By prioritizing data center integration, Alliant Energy seeks to influence regional energy pricing and service reliability positively. Reports suggest that by aligning its investment strategies with long-term power needs, Alliant Energy intends to adopt a disciplined capital approach aimed at achieving its targeted annual earnings growth. This strategy not only mitigates interest rate sensitivity but also capitalizes on the increasing demand for reliable, large-scale power in the Upper Midwest.

The shift towards integrating data centers into its utility operations represents a pivotal recalibration for Alliant Energy, indicating that traditional power providers are now recognizing digital infrastructure as a key driver of grid modernization and long-term financial stability. As the landscape of energy generation continues to evolve, Alliant Energy's strategic focus on these high-growth sectors positions it favorably for future developments in the industry.

- Improvement in gross margin.

- Improvement in asset turnover.

SECTION 2 CORPORATE PROFILE

2.1 Activities

Alliant Energy Corporation is a publicly traded utility holding company listed on the NASDAQ, operating primarily in the Midwest United States through its subsidiaries, Interstate Power and Light Company and Wisconsin Power and Light Company. Its core business activities center on the generation, transmission, distribution, and sale of electricity and natural gas, serving approximately 1 million electric and 420,000 natural gas customers in Wisconsin and Iowa. The company emphasizes reliable energy delivery while prioritizing environmental sustainability and infrastructure modernization. Among its fastest-growing segments are renewable energy initiatives, particularly wind and solar power generation, which have expanded rapidly due to increasing regulatory support, technological advancements, and rising demand for clean energy sources. This strategic focus positions Alliant Energy to enhance its competitive edge in the evolving utility sector, with ongoing investments aimed at reducing carbon emissions and improving operational efficiency. It is the NASDAQ's 5th largest Utility company by market capitalisation.

Alliant Energy AI data center endeavors have taken a significant leap with the establishment of the Beaver Dam AI Data Center. The Alliant Energy Beaver Dam AI Data Center, or BDADC, represents Alliant Energy data center pivot towards powering the Midwest data centers efficiently. With the Meta Alliant Energy partnership, the Meta Beaver Dam AI Data Center utilizes Alliant Energy’s power to ensure operational stability. Recently, Alliant Energy announced a $1 billion equity raise to support these initiatives, reflecting Alliant Energy equity raise ambitions. The Alliant Energy investor reaction has been optimistic, leading to positive Alliant Energy LNT stock news.

Alliant Energy targets data center growth and infrastructure development, ensuring utility stability for data centers. Despite Alliant Energy inflation concerns, the company's focus on Alliant Energy renewable energy projects, namely Alliant Energy wind project Minnesota and Alliant Energy new wind project Minnesota, underscores its commitment to resilient growth. Alliant Energy infrastructure investments and Alliant Energy steady returns offer promising prospects for sustainable energy solutions.

SECTION 3 RECENT NEWS & PAST YEAR TOP 10 STORIES

3.1 RECENT NEWS

Recent News 1 of 5, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 341 words

Tue Apr 21, 2026: Alliant Energy Advances Bent Tree North Wind Project in Minnesota

Alliant Energy has announced that its Bent Tree North Wind project in Freeborn County, Minnesota, will proceed following recent regulatory approvals. The energy company received unanimous backing for the construction of a 150-megawatt (MW) project adjacent to its existing 200-MW Bent Tree wind farm. This approval was granted by both the Minnesota Public Utilities Commission (PUC) and the Public Service Commission of Wisconsin (PSC) on the 21st of April, 2026, marking a significant milestone for Alliant Energy.

Becky Valcq, president of Alliant Energy's Wisconsin energy company, emphasized that the unanimous approvals from regulators in both states reflect strong confidence in the project's potential benefits. "Approval of the Bent Tree North Wind project marks an important step as Alliant Energy continues to build a balanced energy portfolio that includes both renewable and traditional resources to meet customer demand," Valcq stated.

The Bent Tree North Wind project has garnered support from various stakeholders, including the Freeborn County Chamber of Commerce and multiple labor unions. These endorsements highlight the project's potential to stimulate economic growth in the region and enhance the reliability of the energy grid. Valerie Lockhart, executive director of the Albert Lea-Freeborn County Chamber of Commerce, noted, "The Bent Tree North Wind Project represents continued, meaningful investment in Freeborn County—creating jobs, supporting our tax base, and delivering long-term value to local landowners."

Alliant Energy projects that the new wind initiative will generate over $100 million in economic benefits, covering local tax revenue, payments to landowners, construction wages, and other community investments. The company's projections indicate that the project will produce sufficient renewable energy to power approximately 50,000 homes annually, reinforcing its commitment to sustainable energy solutions.

The Bent Tree North initiative is a key component of Alliant Energy's broader Energy Blueprint, which outlines the company's strategy for integrating renewable and traditional energy generation resources. This roadmap aims to provide cost-effective and reliable energy to customers while promoting a balanced energy future. For further details about the Bent Tree North Wind project and Alliant Energy's wind energy initiatives, visit the company's official website.

Recent News 2 of 5, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 315 words

Tue Apr 14, 2026: Alliant Energy's Strategic Moves Position it for Growth in a Shifting Economy

Alliant Energy Corporation is poised for resilient growth as industrial growth shifts toward data centers and infrastructure. This is particularly relevant for U.S. investors looking for defensive investments with built-in inflation protection. On 14th April 2026, Alliant Energy's regulated business model positions the company favorably to meet America's energy demands.

Alliant Energy Corporation, identified by the stock code US0188021085, provides a stake in a regulated utility that supplies electricity and natural gas primarily in Iowa and Wisconsin. Its regulated structure means that the company earns returns sanctioned by state regulatory commissions, shielding it from the economic fluctuations that often impact discretionary sectors more severely. For investors, this translates into a stable business model characterized by predictable cash flows derived from essential services rather than volatile commodity prices.

The utility generates power from a well-balanced mix of coal, natural gas, wind, and solar energy, and it continues to invest in renewable sources to meet environmental mandates. This diversification lessens the risks associated with fuel costs and positions Alliant for federal incentives tied to decarbonization efforts. Historically, U.S. utilities have offered dividends in the range of 3-4%, which adds to their appeal for long-term investors.

Shifts in industrial technology are creating favorable conditions for utilities such as Alliant. Sectors like data centers and infrastructure are becoming increasingly significant consumers of power. The global industrial tech market is projected to experience robust growth, particularly in automation and green technology, which are expected to grow at double-digit CAGRs. This rising demand for reliable baseload power corresponds well with Alliant's capabilities, particularly as hyperscale data centers expand.

Going forward, it will be important for investors to keep an eye on upcoming rate case outcomes and quarterly reports indicating load growth, as well as dividend announcements that reinforce income strategies. Relevant macroeconomic indicators, including Federal Reserve interest rate paths and infrastructure bill implementations, could either strengthen or weaken Alliant's market position.

Recent News 3 of 5, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 363 words

Tue Apr 14, 2026: Alliant Energy's New Strategy Focuses on Powering Data Centers in the Midwest

Alliant Energy Corporation has announced a strategic pivot aimed at addressing the growing electricity demands of hyperscale data centers in the Midwest. On the 13th of April 2026, the company revealed plans to leverage its regulated utility model to ensure grid reliability while targeting an ambitious annual earnings growth of 6% to 8%. This shift reflects a broader strategy to modernize the Midwest energy grid, aligning with the increasing demand for robust digital infrastructure.

Serving over 1 million electric and 400,000 natural gas customers through its subsidiaries, Interstate Power and Light and Wisconsin Power and Light, Alliant Energy is responding to a projected 6% compound annual growth rate in industrial technology through 2030. This strategic adaptation allows Alliant Energy to utilize its regulated utility framework—earning state-approved returns—to mitigate economic volatility while fulfilling the heavy baseload power requirements of modern data centers. By managing a balanced generation portfolio that includes wind, solar, natural gas, and coal, the company aims to ensure a sustainable energy future.

The utility's focus on grid modernization is designed to align with both federal decarbonization mandates and the technical needs of high-capacity data facilities. Analysts view this move as positioning Alliant Energy as a defensive investment, offering shareholders predictable dividends amid significant transitions in the energy market. The company's commitment to integrating digital infrastructure into its utility model reflects a proactive approach to the evolving energy landscape.

By prioritizing data center integration, Alliant Energy seeks to influence regional energy pricing and service reliability positively. Reports suggest that by aligning its investment strategies with long-term power needs, Alliant Energy intends to adopt a disciplined capital approach aimed at achieving its targeted annual earnings growth. This strategy not only mitigates interest rate sensitivity but also capitalizes on the increasing demand for reliable, large-scale power in the Upper Midwest.

The shift towards integrating data centers into its utility operations represents a pivotal recalibration for Alliant Energy, indicating that traditional power providers are now recognizing digital infrastructure as a key driver of grid modernization and long-term financial stability. As the landscape of energy generation continues to evolve, Alliant Energy's strategic focus on these high-growth sectors positions it favorably for future developments in the industry.

Tue Apr 14, 2026: Alliant Energy Corporation Sets Sights on Infrastructure Expansion Amid Rising Demand

Alliant Energy Corporation is aligning its regulated utility model to capture the rising demand from data centers and industrial infrastructure in Iowa and Wisconsin. Market data indicates that industrial technology growth is projected at a 6% compound annual growth rate through 2030. This expansion, largely driven by hyperscaler-led data center builds, is expected to significantly increase electricity needs within Alliant Energy's specific geographic footprint, creating opportunities for future revenue growth.

Alliant Energy maintains a diverse generation mix, which includes wind, solar, natural gas, and coal. This diversification helps reduce fuel cost risks while positioning the company to benefit from federal decarbonization incentives and grid-hardening programs, further enhancing its growth prospects.

Institutional analysts generally maintain hold to buy ratings on Alliant Energy stock, citing its track record of dividend increases and rate base growth. While interest rate sensitivity remains a factor for monitoring, the regulated model is viewed as a hedge against commodity price swings and broader market volatility, ensuring consistent returns for investors.

Recent News 5 of 5, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 321 words

Tue Apr 14, 2026: Alliant Energy Sets Sights on Data Center Demand Amid Utility Stability

Alliant Energy Corp. is strategically positioning itself to address the surging electricity demand from data centers and industrial infrastructure across the Midwest. As of 15th April 2026, the utility provider serves over 1 million electric and 400,000 natural gas customers through its primary subsidiaries, Interstate Power and Light Company in Iowa and Wisconsin Power and Light Company in Wisconsin.

With a regulated utility model, Alliant Energy earns returns that are approved by state commissions, providing a buffer against volatile economic cycles. The company currently manages its energy generation through a balanced portfolio that includes wind, solar, natural gas, and coal. Ongoing capital plans are focused on grid modernization and expanding renewable energy resources to comply with clean energy mandates and capitalize on federal decarbonization incentives.

The demand for steady power is projected to rise significantly, driven by an anticipated 6% compound annual growth rate in industrial technology through 2030. According to industry experts, utilities like Alliant Energy anchor their portfolios by concentrating on essential infrastructure, particularly during periods of market uncertainty. The emergence of hyperscaler-driven data centers in the Upper Midwest necessitates reliable baseload power, a demand that aligns with Alliant Energy's regional capabilities.

This structural shift in energy demand supports Alliant Energy's disciplined capital approach, which aims for an annual earnings growth of 6% to 8%. Analysts generally hold a favorable view of the company due to its rate base growth and a consistent track record of dividends. Despite concerns regarding interest rate sensitivity, Alliant Energy's low-leverage strategy is designed to outpace inflation while ensuring service reliability, further solidifying its position in the market.

As Alliant Energy continues to navigate the evolving energy landscape, its focus on data center growth not only enhances its utility stability but also positions the company for sustainable long-term success. With a commitment to meeting the energy needs of the future, Alliant Energy is poised to play a critical role in the region's economic development.

3.2 PAST YEAR TOP 10 STORIES

Story 1 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 90 words

Alliant Energy Q3 2025: Narrowed Guidance and Dividend Growth Driven by Data Center Demand

Alliant Energy's third-quarter 2025 financial report highlights narrowed earnings guidance of $3.17-$3.23 per share and a projected 6.6% annual growth for 2026 earnings. The company also announced a 5.4% dividend increase, reflecting confidence in its financial model. A key driver for this positive outlook is the utility's contracted peak demand of 3 gigawatts from data centers, which is expected to increase peak load by 50% by 2030. To support this growth, Alliant raised its four-year capital expenditure forecast by 17% to $13.4 billion, focusing on renewable energy and infrastructure modernization.

Story 2 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 82 words

Alliant Energy Positions for 50% Peak Load Growth Through 3 Gigawatts of Contracted Data Center Demand

Alliant Energy has secured 3 gigawatts of contracted peak demand from hyperscale data center customers, signaling a transformative growth trajectory that will increase total system peak load by 50% by 2030. Major projects from QTS Data Centers, Google, and Meta have bolstered the company's pipeline, validating a strategic shift toward data center-supporting infrastructure. To meet this demand, Alliant plans significant expansions in natural gas capacity, energy storage, and wind generation through 2030, while safe-harboring capital expenditures to protect against tax law changes.

Story 3 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 80 words

Alliant Energy Increases Capital Investment Plan 17% to $13.4 Billion Through 2029

To support a projected 50% peak load growth by 2030, Alliant Energy has increased its capital expenditure plan for 2026-2029 by 17% to $13.4 billion. The investment prioritizes renewable energy, energy storage, natural gas generation, and electric distribution modernization. Approximately 85% of this plan supports data center-related infrastructure. The company has implemented "safe harbor" tax treatments for planned expenditures through 2028 and intends to fund the program through a mix of operations cash, debt, tax credit monetization, and equity capital.

Story 4 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 65 words

Wisconsin Public Service Commission Approves Unanimous Rate Settlement: $57.8 Million Reduction from Initial Request

The Wisconsin Public Service Commission unanimously approved a rate settlement for Alliant Energy, reducing the company's initial electric rate increase request by $57.8 million. The agreement lowers the 2026 electric rate increase to 5.4% and the 2027 increase to 5.0%. The settlement balances capital investment needs with ratepayer affordability and includes commitments to renewable energy programs, expanded Time-of-Use rates, and improved distributed solar interconnection processes.

Story 5 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 79 words

Google Announces Second Iowa Data Center at Big Cedar Industrial Center with $7 Billion State Investment Commitment

Google confirmed plans for a second data center at Alliant Energy's Big Cedar Industrial Center in Cedar Rapids, Iowa, accompanied by a $7 billion state investment commitment. The project involves a $576 million direct construction investment and is expected to create hundreds of construction jobs and over 30 permanent positions. This development, alongside QTS Data Centers' expansion, establishes the region as a major AI infrastructure hub. Alliant Energy is contributing to supporting infrastructure improvements, including water and sewer expansions.

Story 6 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 72 words

Meta Announces $1 Billion Beaver Dam Data Center with $200 Million Energy Infrastructure Partnership

Meta announced a $1 billion investment in a new data center campus in Beaver Dam, Wisconsin, slated for operation in 2027. The project includes a $200 million partnership with Alliant Energy for essential energy infrastructure upgrades. The facility is expected to create approximately 1,000 construction jobs and 100 operational roles. Meta also committed to environmental stewardship, including water restoration and habitat preservation, and pledged financial support to Alliant's Hometown Care Energy Fund.

Story 7 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 75 words

Alliant Energy Activates Grant County Battery Storage and Advances Lansing 280-Megawatt Project

Alliant Energy has activated a 100-megawatt Battery Energy Storage System (BESS) in Grant County, Wisconsin, and is advancing construction on a 280-megawatt facility in Lansing, Iowa, expected to be operational by late 2026. These projects, along with a 75-megawatt installation in Franklin County, are part of Alliant's strategy to deploy 800 megawatts of battery storage by 2030. The storage systems are crucial for managing grid stability and peak load demand driven by data center growth.

Story 8 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 64 words

Nordex Awarded Major Wind Turbine Supply Contract: 190 Turbines for 1,060 Megawatts

Nordex Group has been awarded a supply contract by Alliant Energy for up to 190 wind turbines, totaling 1,060 megawatts of capacity for installation in 2028-2029. This agreement, contingent upon regulatory approval, represents Nordex's largest U.S. contract and utilizes its Iowa manufacturing facility. The project supports Alliant's 1,200-megawatt wind expansion target to meet data center demand and decarbonization goals, while promoting regional economic development.

Story 9 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 75 words

Alliant Energy Q1 2025: 33.9% EPS Growth and Updated $11.5 Billion Capital Plan

Alliant Energy reported a 33.9% increase in earnings per share for the first quarter of 2025, driven by revenue from capital investments and favorable weather impacts. The company updated its four-year capital expenditure plan to $11.5 billion, a 26% increase aimed at supporting an 11% compound annual growth rate in its rate base. This capital allocation focuses on renewables, storage, and grid modernization to support data center demand, with funding secured through diverse financial strategies.

Story 10 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 61 words

Alliant Energy & QTS Partnership: 750-Megawatt Renewable Energy Credit Agreement

Alliant Energy and QTS Data Centers announced an agreement in principle for the sale of approximately 750 megawatts of renewable energy credits (RECs). This partnership allows QTS to meet sustainability goals through third-party generation purchases while enabling Alliant to monetize surplus credits and lower capital costs for new renewable projects. Subject to regulatory approval, the deal aligns with Alliant's strategy to.

MILWAUKEE, Thursday, May 14 2026 - WEC Energy Group, Inc's (NYSE: WEC $111.65) stock price hit a 52-week high of $111.73 during the day. In the last three months the stock has hit a new 52-week high seven times, pointing to a significant uptrend.

WEC Energy Group, Inc's stock price increased 40.25c (0.4%) from its previous trading session to close at $111.65.

Compared with the S&P 500 Index which rose 61.5 points (0.8%) in the day, the relative price change was -0.5%.

There were 1,139,941 shares worth $127.3 million traded today.

WEC Energy Group, Inc. (NYSE:WEC) is a leading regulated utility company specializing in electricity and natural gas services across the Midwest. The company’s core operations encompass the delivery of reliable electricity and gas supply through advanced infrastructure projects, including transmission upgrades and grid modernization. Committed to innovation, WEC Energy focuses on renewable energy integration and battery energy storage solutions, enhancing their environmental sustainability. Strategic initiatives such as EV charging infrastructure and smart grid AMI support long-term growth. With projects like infrastructure upgrades and robust demand response programs, WEC Energy maintains a strong competitive position in the utility sector.

APPENDIX II STOCK IDENTIFIERS

SECTION 1 CORPORATE PROFILE

1.1 Activities

WEC Energy Group, Inc., a Fortune 500 company listed on the New York Stock Exchange, is a leading energy services provider in the United States, primarily operating in Wisconsin and surrounding regions. Its core business activities encompass the generation, transmission, and distribution of electricity, along with natural gas delivery, serving over 4.6 million customers through subsidiaries like We Energies and Wisconsin Public Service. The company focuses on reliable, affordable, and sustainable energy solutions, with a diversified portfolio that includes fossil fuels, nuclear, and renewables. Among its fastest-growing segments are renewable energy initiatives, such as wind and solar projects, which have expanded rapidly due to increasing demand for clean power, supportive regulations, and corporate sustainability goals. This growth is evidenced by significant investments in wind farms and solar installations, positioning WEC Energy to meet rising environmental standards while enhancing long-term operational efficiency and shareholder value. It is the NYSE's 7th largest Utility company by market capitalisation.

WEC Energy Group, Inc (NYSE:WEC) is a prominent utility company, primarily focused on providing reliable energy services. The WEC Energy Group stock, commonly referred to as WEC stock, reflects its position in energy infrastructure. Facing WEC Energy capex risk, the company is committed to robust WEC Energy capital expenditure to achieve WEC Energy grid upgrades, targeted WEC Energy infrastructure spending, and WEC Energy transmission upgrades. Its plan for WEC Energy distribution modernization includes enhancing WEC Energy reliability for data centers and addressing WEC Energy critical power solutions through WEC Energy interconnection services and WEC Energy large commercial power. With a focus on WEC Energy wholesale power contracts, WEC Energy rate cases present potential WEC Energy regulatory risk. The WEC Energy earnings outlook supports its WEC Energy dividend sustainability and strengthens the WEC Energy investment thesis. Additionally, its commitment to WEC Energy renewable energy and WEC Energy battery storage complements its WEC Energy smart grid initiatives. The strategy, including WEC Energy infrastructure projects in Wisconsin and a robust WEC Energy corporate strategy with data centers, underscores WEC Energy ESG efforts. The anticipated WEC Energy rate base growth is driven by WEC Energy power supply agreements and the company's focus on utility-scale projects, further emphasized by WEC Energy capex guidance.

SECTION 2 RECENT NEWS, PAST YEAR TOP 10 STORIES & PAST MONTH ECONOMIC NEWS

2.1 RECENT NEWS

Recent News 1 of 2, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 288 words

Tue May 12, 2026: WEC Energy Group Secures Board Approval Amid Governance Rejections

Shareholders of WEC Energy Group, Inc. (NYSE:WEC) voted on several proposals at the company's annual meeting held Thursday. On 13th May 2026, all twelve board nominees were elected to serve terms expiring in 2027.

The directors elected include Warner L. Baxter, Ave M. Bie, Danny L. Cunningham, William M. Farrow III, Cristina A. Garcia-Thomas, Maria C. Green, Thomas K. Lane, John D. Lange, Scott J. Lauber, Ulice Payne, Jr., Mary Ellen Stanek, and Glen E. Tellock. Each nominee received more votes in favor than against, signaling shareholder confidence in the current leadership.

In addition to the board elections, shareholders ratified Deloitte & Touche LLP as the company's independent auditor for 2026, with 272,998,864 votes in favor. The advisory vote on executive compensation also saw approval, with 236,230,392 votes supporting the remuneration of named executive officers. WEC has a strong track record, having raised its dividend for 22 consecutive years, currently offering a yield of 3.38%. However, analysts indicate that the stock may be overvalued relative to its Fair Value.

Despite the positive outcomes for board nominees and auditor approval, three proposals concerning corporate governance were rejected. These included amendments to the company's Restated Articles of Incorporation and Bylaws aimed at eliminating supermajority voting requirements. A separate stockholder proposal advocating for governance by majority vote also did not pass, receiving only 94,181,325 votes in favor against 156,193,461 opposed.

In other developments, WEC Energy Group reported first-quarter earnings for 2026 that exceeded Wall Street expectations. The company achieved an earnings per share (EPS) of $2.45, surpassing the forecasted $2.09, alongside revenue of $3.4 billion compared to an anticipated $3.37 billion. These results reflect a strong start to the year, bolstering investor confidence and indicating the effectiveness of WEC's strategic direction.

Recent News 2 of 2, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 294 words

Tue May 5, 2026: WEC Energy Group Announces $15 Billion Vantage Investment in Data Center Project

WEC Energy Group, Inc. has revealed that Vantage is poised to invest $15 billion to complete the initial phase of its extensive data center project by 2028. The announcement was made during a conference call held on the 6th of May 2026 at 18:24:09.815772, signaling a significant commitment to expand the company's infrastructure in the growing data center market.

The planned investment aligns with WEC Energy Group's strategic objectives to enhance its service offerings and improve its operational capabilities. Vantage's substantial funding is expected to bolster the development of cutting-edge facilities designed to meet the increasing demand for data processing and storage solutions amid the digital transformation sweeping across various industries.

WEC Energy Group emphasized that the initial phase of the project will focus on establishing robust data center infrastructure, which is critical for supporting emerging technologies and services. This initiative is part of a broader trend within the energy sector, where companies are increasingly investing in sustainable and efficient energy solutions to power data centers, reflecting a growing commitment to environmental responsibility.

The company's focus on enhancing its environmental, social, and governance practices is underscored by its MSCI ESG score, which assesses these practices relative to industry peers. As WEC Energy Group moves forward with this $15 billion investment, stakeholders will be keenly observing how the company integrates sustainability into its operations and the overall impact on its financial health and profitability.

In conclusion, WEC Energy Group's announcement of Vantage's $15 billion investment is a strategic move that not only positions the company for future growth in the data center space but also highlights its commitment to sustainability and innovation. Investors will likely be watching closely as the project progresses, eager to see how it influences the company's market position and financial performance.

2.2 PAST YEAR TOP 10 STORIES

Story 1 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 87 words

Strong Third-Quarter Results and Accelerated Capital Plan Signal Data-Center-Driven Growth

WEC Energy reported a 9.3% increase in third-quarter net income to USD 0.83 per share, supported by solid revenue growth and a rise in retail electricity deliveries. Concurrently, the company unveiled an expanded USD 36.5 billion five-year capital plan through 2030 to support anticipated demand from data centers and infrastructure upgrades. Management reaffirmed 2025 earnings guidance and projected long-term EPS growth of 7-8%, emphasizing a strategy to insulate existing customers from costs via specific tariffs while pivoting toward a growth platform driven by AI and cloud computing.

Story 2 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 95 words

Oracle and OpenAI Anchor USD 15 Billion Vantage Lighthouse Campus in Port Washington

Vantage Data Centers announced a USD 15 billion AI campus in Port Washington, Wisconsin, anchored by OpenAI and Oracle. The project, expected to consume 1.3 gigawatts of power, will be supported by a dedicated tariff structure with We Energies, ensuring Vantage funds 100% of the necessary infrastructure investment. This arrangement protects other ratepayers while allocating 70% of new clean energy capacity to the campus. The development is projected to create thousands of jobs and contribute significantly to regional GDP, marking a major step in WEC's role as a critical infrastructure provider for the tech sector.

Story 3 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 74 words

Microsoft Announces Additional USD 4 Billion Wisconsin Data Center Investment, Raising Total State Commitment to USD 7 Billion

Microsoft committed an additional USD 4 billion to its data center operations in Mount Pleasant, Wisconsin, raising its total state investment to over USD 7 billion. This expansion reinforces WEC Energy's demand growth assumptions, suggesting current estimates may be conservative. Microsoft plans to pre-fund electrical upgrades and utilize advanced cooling to minimize water usage. While this solidifies WEC's position in AI infrastructure, it introduces execution risks regarding timely infrastructure delivery and regulatory cost allocations.

Story 4 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 78 words

Public Service Commission of Wisconsin Approves More Than 450 Megawatts of Large-Scale Renewable Energy and Storage Projects

The Public Service Commission of Wisconsin unanimously approved We Energies' acquisition of over 450 MW of renewable energy and storage projects, set for completion by 2028. This approval supports WEC's decarbonization goals and its ability to meet the renewable energy requirements of hyperscale data center clients. The company is also pursuing further approvals for nearly 3 GW of additional capacity. While supply chain and labor risks exist, the regulatory support strengthens WEC's "all of the above" generation strategy.

Story 5 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 80 words

U.S. Department of Energy Issues Conditional USD 2.5 Billion Loan Guarantee for Renewable Energy and Storage Projects

The DOE announced a conditional USD 2.5 billion loan guarantee to a WEC Energy subsidiary to finance renewable energy and storage projects in Wisconsin. This funding lowers the cost of capital for critical infrastructure needed to serve data centers and aligns with national development priorities. The loan is expected to reduce pressure on customer rates, though it remains subject to finalization and compliance with federal conditions. WEC has committed to community benefit plans, including labor engagement, to meet loan requirements.

Story 6 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 75 words

Strong First-Quarter Earnings with Robust Revenue Growth and Expanded Rate Base

WEC Energy's first-quarter 2025 net income rose 15.2% to USD 2.27 per share, driven by favorable weather and rate base growth from a recent regulatory review. Revenues increased 17.5% to USD 3.1 billion. The company reaffirmed its full-year 2025 earnings guidance and long-term dividend growth targets. Despite higher operating costs associated with the expanded rate base, the strong start to the year de-risks the annual outlook, with management demonstrating effective cost discipline and operational execution.

Story 7 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 66 words

Second-Quarter Earnings Demonstrate Continued Growth and Elevated Demand Trends

Second-quarter 2025 net income increased 13.4% to USD 0.76 per share, contributing to a first-half earnings rise to USD 3.02 per share. Results were bolstered by utility operations and rate-based growth, despite headwinds from storm damage. Management reaffirmed full-year 2025 guidance and long-term growth targets, citing consistent execution and broad-based demand strength. The report highlights the company's resilience to adverse weather events while maintaining capital discipline.

Story 8 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 74 words

Board Authorizes 6.7% Dividend Increase and Introduces 2026 Earnings Guidance

The board authorized a 6.7% dividend increase effective Q1 2026 and introduced 2026 earnings guidance of USD 5.51-5.61 per share, implying 6.1% growth. These moves reflect confidence in the company's ability to fund its capital plan while delivering predictable shareholder returns. The dividend policy remains aligned with a target payout ratio of 65-70%. Risks include execution of the capital plan and regulatory approvals, but the announcement reinforces WEC's status as a stable, growth-oriented utility.

Story 9 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 75 words

Regulatory Filings and Progress on Customer-Specific Tariff Structures for Hyperscale Data Centers

WEC Energy is advancing regulatory proceedings for bespoke tariff structures designed to insulate residential and small business customers from the costs of serving hyperscale data centers. The framework ensures large customers like Vantage Data Centers underwrite 100% of their specific infrastructure investments. This strategy allows WEC to pursue growth opportunities without political risk regarding rate impacts. While subject to potential appeals, the approach signifies a strategic evolution in regulatory engagement to accommodate large-load customers equitably.

Story 10 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 74 words

Strong Full-Year 2024 Results and Reaffirmed 2025 Earnings Guidance Position Company for Multi-Year Growth

Following strong full-year 2024 results that beat expectations, WEC Energy reaffirmed its 2025 earnings guidance of USD 5.17-5.27 per share. Management expressed confidence in the company's structural quality and ability to navigate an expanded capital plan and evolving regulatory landscape. The consistent guidance signals effective risk management and operational execution. Institutional investors view the reaffirmation as validation of WEC's strategic direction, with continued focus on executing major projects and managing data center load growth.

2.3 ECONOMIC NEWS

ECO NEWS 1 of 1, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 217 words

Apr 22, 2026: Anterix Executes 10 MHz Benton PUD Spectrum Sale, Extending Utility Private-LTE Reference Base Relevant to WEC Energy Group's Communications Infrastructure Planning

On April 21, 2026, Anterix Inc., via subsidiary PDV Spectrum Holding Company, LLC, signed a $771,000 agreement to sell a 10 MHz 900 MHz broadband spectrum license to Public Utility District No. 1 of Benton County in Washington. Industry reports indicate the utility-owned, utility-controlled network will cover a 927-square-mile service territory with more than 58,000 customers.

Market data indicates the transaction marks the first private wireless broadband deployment among publicly owned utilities in the Pacific Northwest. The deployment enables grid automation, field communications, outage management, and resilience aligned with state clean-energy requirements.

Impact on WEC Energy Group: WEC

Following this development, WEC Energy Group observes an expanded peer-utility reference base relevant to its communications infrastructure planning across Wisconsin, Illinois, Michigan, and Minnesota. Operational metrics indicate WEC serves approximately 4.7 million combined electric and natural gas customers through operating subsidiaries including We Energies, Wisconsin Public Service, and Peoples Gas, and has been advancing grid-modernization investments focused on reliability, advanced metering, and distribution automation.

Analysis shows the Benton PUD agreement expands the national pool of utilities deploying Anterix 900 MHz private LTE, strengthening the vendor ecosystem and shared operational knowledge relevant to any future WEC private-wireless investment decisions.

urn:contentItem:6JH3-WV23-RX39-N3BX-00000-00Renewable Energy Stocks To Consider - May 4th2026-05-04T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:18:41ZLexisNexisRenewable Energy Stocks To Consider - May 4th

The Nasdaq's Historic Rally Doesn't Mean the Risk Is Gone

Quanta Services, WEC Energy Group, and NOV are the three Renewable Energy stocks to watch today, according to MarketBeat's stock screener tool. Renewable energy stocks are shares of companies whose primary business is generating, manufacturing equipment for, developing projects in, or providing services and technologies related to clean energy sources for example solar, wind, hydro, geothermal, biomass, and energy storage. Investors buy these equities to gain exposure to the transition away from fossil fuels and potential long-term growth, while accepting risks tied to policy changes, subsidies, technology shifts, and project or commodity cycles. These companies had the highest dollar trading volume of any Renewable Energy stocks within the last several days.

Quanta Services PWR

Quanta Services, Inc. provides infrastructure solutions for the electric and gas utility, renewable energy, communications, and pipeline and energy industries in the United States, Canada, Australia, and internationally. The company's Electric Power Infrastructure Solutions segment engages in the design, procurement, construction, upgrade, repair, and maintenance of electric power transmission and distribution infrastructure and substation facilities; installation, maintenance, and upgrade of electric power infrastructure projects; installation of smart grid technologies on electric power networks; and design, installation, maintenance, and repair of commercial and industrial wirings.

The Power Bill, the AI Dip, and the Date That Could Flip 2026 Stocks

Read Our Latest Research Report on PWR

WEC Energy Group WEC

WEC Energy Group, Inc., through its subsidiaries, provides regulated natural gas and electricity, and renewable and nonregulated renewable energy services in the United States. It operates through Wisconsin, Illinois, Other States, Electric Transmission, and Non-Utility Energy Infrastructure segments.

Quanta Services: The Backbone of the AI Data Center Push

Read Our Latest Research Report on WEC

NOV NOV

NOV Inc. designs, constructs, manufactures, and sells systems, components, and products for oil and gas drilling and production, and industrial and renewable energy sectors in the United States and internationally. It operates through two segments, Energy Equipment, and Energy Products and Services. The company provides solids control and waste management equipment and services, managed pressure drilling, drilling fluids, premium drillpipe, wired pipe, drilling optimization services, tubular inspection and coating services, instrumentation, downhole tools, and drill bits.

urn:contentItem:6JJ0-DWP3-RWYX-53NB-00000-00Promising Renewable Energy Stocks To Follow Now - May 7th2026-05-09T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:18:41ZLexisNexisPromising Renewable Energy Stocks To Follow Now - May 7th
